verses.text: And Yahweh spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai in the tent of meeting on one of the second month in the second year of their going out from the land of Egypt, saying,
“Lift up the head of all the congregation of the sons of Israel, according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, every male according to their skulls,
from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war in Israel. Count them, you and Aaron, according to their armies.
And with you will be a man, a man of the tribe. He will be the head man of the house of his fathers.
And these are the names of the men who will stand with you: of Reuben, Elizur, the son of Shedeur,
of Simeon, Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai;
of Judah, Nahshon, the son of Amminadab;
of Issachar, Nethanel, the son of Zuar;
of Zebulun, Eliab, the son of Helon;
of the sons of Joseph: of Ephraim, Elishama, the son of Ammihud; of Manasseh, Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur;
of Benjamin, Abidan, the son of Gideoni;
of Dan, Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai;
of Asher, Pagiel, the son of Okran;
of Gad, Eliasaph, the son of Deuel;
of Naphtali, Ahira, the son of Enan.”
These were the called ones of the congregation, the leaders of the tribes of their fathers. They were the heads of the thousands of Israel.
And Moses and Aaron took these men, who were designated by names.

And they assembled all the congregation on one of the second month. And they recorded their ancestry in accordance with their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, according to their skulls,
just as Yahweh had commanded Moses. And he counted them in the wilderness of Sinai.

And they were the sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, according to their skulls, every male from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Reuben: 46,500.

Of the sons of Simeon, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, his counted ones by the number of the names, according to their skulls, every male from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Simeon were 59,300.
Of the sons of Gad, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Gad were 45,650.
Of the sons of Judah, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Judah were 74,600.

Of the sons of Issachar, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Issachar were 54,400.

Of the sons of Zebulun, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Zebulun were 57,400.

Of the sons of Joseph: of the sons of Ephraim, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Ephraim were 40,500.

Of the sons of Manasseh, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Manasseh were 32,200.

Of the sons of Benjamin, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Benjamin were 35,400.
Of the sons of Dan, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Dan were 62,700.
Of the sons of Asher, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Asher were 41,500.

Of the sons of Naphtali, their generations according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, by the number of the names, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war,
their counted ones of the tribe of Naphtali were 53,400.
These were the counted ones, whom Moses and Aaron had counted, and the leaders of Israel, 12 men. They were one man for the house of his fathers.
And they were all the counted ones of the sons of Israel according to the house of their fathers, from a son of 20 years and upward, everyone who goes out to war in Israel.
And all the counted ones were 603,550.
And the Levites according to the tribe of their fathers were not counted in the midst of them.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Only the tribe of Levi you shall not count, and you shall not lift up their heads in the midst of the sons of Israel.

And you, appoint the Levites over the Dwelling of the Testimony and over all its vessels and over all that belongs to it. They themselves shall carry the Dwelling and all its vessels, and they themselves shall minister to it, and they shall camp around the Dwelling.
And when the Dwelling sets out, the Levites shall take it down, and when the Dwelling camps, the Levites shall raise it up. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.
And the sons of Israel shall camp, a man at his camp and a man at his banner, according to their armies.

And the Levites shall camp around the Dwelling of the Testimony, and wrath will not be on the congregation of the sons of Israel. And the Levites shall keep the keeping of the Dwelling of the Testimony.”
And the sons of Israel did according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so they did.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
“The sons of Israel will camp, a man at his banner, with the sign of the house of their fathers. They will camp from the front, around the tent of meeting.
And the ones camping to the east, from the sunrise, will be the banner of the camp of Judah according to their armies, and the leader of the sons of Judah is Nahshon, the son of Amminadab,
and his army and their counted ones are 74,600.
And the ones camping beside him will be the tribe of Issachar, and the leader of the sons of Issachar is Nethanel, the son of Zuar,
and his army and his counted ones are 54,400.
The tribe of Zebulun: and the leader of the sons of Zebulun is Eliab, the son of Helon,
and his army and his counted ones are 57,400.
All the counted ones of the camp of Judah are 186, 400 according to their armies. They will set out first.
The banner of the camp of Reuben will be to the south according to their armies, and the leader of the sons of Reuben is Elizur, the son of Shedeur,
and his army and his counted ones are 46,500.
And the ones camping beside him will be the tribe of Simeon, and the leader of the sons of Simeon is Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai,
and his army and their counted ones are 59,300.
And the tribe of Gad: and the leader of the sons of Gad is Eliasaph, the son of Reuel,
and his army and their counted ones are 45,650.
All the counted ones of the camp of Reuben are 151,450 according to their armies. And they will set out second.
And the tent of meeting will set out with the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps. As they camp, so they will set out, a man on his hand, according to their banners.
The banner of the camp of Ephraim according to their armies will be to the west, and the leader of the sons of Ephraim is Elishama, the son of Ammihud,
and his army and their counted ones are 40,500.
And beside him will be the tribe of Manasseh, and the leader of the sons of Manasseh is Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur,
and his army and their counted ones are 32,200.
And the tribe of Benjamin: and the leader of the sons of Benjamin is Abidan, the son of Gideoni,
and his army and their counted ones are 35,400.
All the counted ones of the camp of Ephraim are 108,100 according to their armies. And they will set out third.
The banner of the camp of Dan will be to the north according to their armies, and the leader of the sons of Dan is Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai,
and his army and their counted ones are 62,700.
And the ones camping beside him will be the tribe of Asher, and the leader of the sons of Asher is Pagiel, the son of Okran,
and his army and their counted ones are 41,500.
And the tribe of Naphtali: and the leader of the sons of Naphtali is Ahira, the son of Enan,
and his army and their counted ones are 53,400.
All the counted ones of the camp of Dan are 157,600. They will set out last, according to their banners.”
These are the counted ones of the sons of Israel according to the house of their fathers. All the counted ones of the camps according to their armies are 603,550.
And they did not count the Levites in the midst of the sons of Israel, just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.

And the sons of Israel did according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses. Thus they camped according to their banners, and thus they set out, a man according to his clans, in accordance with the house of his fathers.
And these are the generations of Aaron and Moses on the day Yahweh spoke with Moses on the mountain of Sinai.
And these are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
These are the names of the sons of Aaron, the priests, the ones who were anointed, whose hand he filled to serve as priest.

But Nadab died and Abihu to the face of Yahweh when they offered strange fire to the face of Yahweh in the wilderness of Sinai, and sons were not to them. And Eleazar served as priest and Ithamar against the face of Aaron their father.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Bring near the tribe of Levi and station it to the face of Aaron the priest. And they shall minister to him.
And they shall keep his keeping and the keeping of all the congregation to the face of the tent of meeting, to serve the service of the Dwelling.
And they shall keep all the vessels of the tent of meeting and the keeping of the sons of Israel, to serve the service of the Dwelling.
And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and to his sons. They are given, given to him from with the sons of Israel.
And you shall count Aaron and his sons, and they shall keep their priesthood. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“And I, behold, I have taken the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel instead of every firstborn, the opener of the womb from the sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be for me,

for every firstborn is for me. On the day of my striking down every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated for me every firstborn in Israel, from man up to animal. They are for me. I am Yahweh.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, saying,
“Count the sons of Levi according to the house of their fathers, according to their clans. You shall count them, every male from a son of a month and upward.”
And Moses counted them upon the mouth of Yahweh, just as he was commanded.
And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon and Kohath and Merari.
And these are the names of the sons of Gershon according to their clans: Libni and Shimei.
And the sons of Kohath according to their clans were Amram and Izhar, Hebron and Uzziel.
And the sons of Merari according to their clans were Mahli and Mushi. These are they, the clans of the Levites according to the house of their fathers.
Of Gershon were the clan of the Libnite and the clan of the Shimeite. These are they, the clans of the Gershonite.
Their counted ones by the number of every male from a son of a month and upward, their counted ones were 7,500.
The clans of the Gershonite camp behind the Dwelling, to the west.
And the leader of the house of the father for the Gershonite was Eliasaph, the son of Lael.
And the keeping of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting was the Dwelling and the tent, its covering and the curtain of the opening of the tent of meeting
and the hangings of the courtyard and the curtain of the opening of the courtyard that is on the Dwelling and on the altar all around, and its ropes for all its service.
And of Kohath were the clan of the Amramite and the clan of the Izharite and the clan of the Hebronite and the clan of the Uzzielite. These are they, the clans of the Kohathite.
By the number of every male from a son of a month and upward, 8,600 were the keepers of the keeping of the holy place.
The clans of the sons of Kohath camp on the side of the Dwelling to the south.
And the leader of the house of the father for the clans of the Kohathite was Elizaphan, the son of Uzziel.
And their keeping was the box and the table and the lampstand and the altars and the vessels of the holy place with which they minister and the curtain, and all its service.
And the leader of the leaders of the Levites was Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, the oversight of the keepers of the keeping of the holy place.
Of Merari were the clan of the Mahlite and the clan of the Mushite. These are they, the clans of Merari.
And their counted ones by the number of every male from a son of a month and upward were 6,200.
And the leader of the house of the father for the clans of Merari was Zuriel, the son of Abihail. They camp on the side of the Dwelling to the north.
And the oversight of the keeping of the sons of Merari was the frames of the Dwelling and its crossbars and its pillars and its bases and all its vessels, and all its service,
and the pillars of the courtyard all around and their bases and their pegs and their ropes.

And the ones camping to the face of the Dwelling to the east, to the face of the tent of meeting toward the sunrise, were Moses and Aaron and his sons, the keepers of the keeping of the holy place, according to the keeping of the sons of Israel. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.
All the counted ones of the Levites whom Moses and Aaron counted upon the mouth of Yahweh, according to their clans, every male from a son of a month and upward, were 22,000.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Count every firstborn male of the sons of Israel from a son of a month and upward. And lift up the number of their names.

And you shall take the Levites for me—I am Yahweh—instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the animal of the Levites instead of every firstborn among the animal of the sons of Israel.”
And Moses counted, just as Yahweh had commanded him, all the firstborn among the sons of Israel.
And it happened that every firstborn male by the number of the names from a son of a month and upward, according to their counted ones, was 22,273.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Take the Levites instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel, and the animal of the Levites instead of their animal. And the Levites shall be for me—I am Yahweh.
And the ransoms of the 273, the ones being in excess over the Levites from the firstborn of the sons of Israel,
and you shall take five, five shekels for the skull. You shall take by the shekel of the holy place (20 gerahs is the shekel).
And you shall give the silver to Aaron and to his sons, the ransoms of the ones being in excess among them.”
And Moses took the silver of the ransom from with the ones being in excess over the ransoms of the Levites.
From with the firstborn of the sons of Israel he took the silver: 1,365 by the shekel of the holy place.
And Moses gave the silver of the ransoms to Aaron and to his sons upon the mouth of Yahweh, just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
“Lift up the head of the sons of Kohath from the midst of the sons of Levi, according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers,
from a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years, every one who comes to the army to do work in the tent of meeting.
This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting, the holy of the holies.
And Aaron shall come and his sons when the camp sets out, and they shall take down the veil of the curtain, and with it they shall cover the Box of the Testimony.
And they shall put on it a covering of skin of a porpoise, and they shall spread a cloth of completely blue from above, and they shall set its poles.

And over the table of the faces they shall spread a cloth of blue, and they shall put on it the dishes and the palm-sized bowls and the bowls and the pitchers of the drink offering, and the bread of continuity shall be on it.
And they shall spread over them a cloth of worm of scarlet, and they shall cover it with a covering of skin of a porpoise, and they shall set its poles.
And they shall take a cloth of blue, and they shall cover the lampstand of the light and its lamps and its tongs and its trays and all the vessels of its oil with which they minister to it.
And they shall put it and all its vessels into a covering of skin of a porpoise, and they shall put them on the carrying pole.
And over the altar of gold they shall spread a cloth of blue, and they shall cover it with a covering of skin of a porpoise, and they shall set its poles.

And they shall take all the vessels of the ministry with which they minister in the holy place, and they shall put them into a cloth of blue. And they shall cover them with a covering of skin of a porpoise. And they shall put them on the carrying pole.
And they shall remove the ashes from the altar, and they shall spread over it a cloth of purple.

And they shall put on it all its vessels with which they minister on it: the trays, the meat forks and the shovels and the basins—all the vessels of the altar. And they shall spread over it a covering of skin of a porpoise, and they shall set its poles.

And Aaron and his sons shall finish to cover the holy place and all the vessels of the holy place when the camp sets out. And after that the sons of Kohath shall come to carry, and they shall not touch the holy place and die. These are the burden of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting.

And the oversight of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, is the oil of the light and the incense of the spices and the grain offering of continuity and the oil of the anointing, the oversight of all the Dwelling and all that is in it, in the holy place, and in its vessels.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
“Do not cut off the tribe of the clans of the Kohathite from the midst of the Levites.
But this do for them, and they will live and not die when they come near the holy of the holies: Aaron and his sons shall go and set them man by man in accordance with his service and to his burden.
But they shall not go to see, when swallowing, the holy place and die.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Lift up the head of the sons of Gershon, also them, according to the house of their fathers, according to their clans.
From a son of 30 years and upward up to a son of 50 years you shall count them, everyone who comes to fight in the army, to serve service in the tent of meeting.
This is the service of the clans of the Gershonite, to serve and according to a burden.
And they shall carry the curtains of the Dwelling and the tent of meeting, its covering and the covering of porpoise which is on it from above and the curtain of the opening of the tent of meeting

and the hangings of the courtyard and the curtain of the opening of the gate of the courtyard that is on the Dwelling and on the altar all around, and their ropes and all the vessels of their service and all that is done for them; and they shall serve.

Upon the mouth of Aaron and his sons will be all the service of the sons of the Gershonite, according to all their burden and according to all their service. And you shall count upon them all their burden in keeping.
This is the service of the clans of the sons of the Gershonite in the tent of meeting, and their keeping is in the hand of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.
The sons of Merari, according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, you shall count them.
From a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years you shall count them, everyone who comes to the army to serve the service of the tent of meeting.
And this is the keeping of their burden, according to all their service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the Dwelling and its crossbars and its pillars and its bases

and the pillars of the courtyard all around and their bases and their pegs and their ropes, for all their vessels and for all their service; and by names you shall count the vessels of the keeping of their burden.
This is the service of the clans of the sons of Merari for all their service in the tent of meeting in the hand of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.”
And Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation counted the sons of the Kohathite according to their clans and according to the house of their fathers,
from a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years, everyone who comes to the army for service in the tent of meeting,
and their counted ones according to their clans were 2,750.
These were the counted ones of the clans of the Kohathite, everyone who serves in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron counted upon the mouth of Yahweh in the hand of Moses.
And the counted ones of the sons of Gershon, according to their clans and according to the house of their fathers,
from a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years, everyone who comes to the army for service in the tent of meeting,
and their counted ones according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers, were 2,630.
These were the counted ones of the clans of the sons of Gershon, everyone who serves in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron counted upon the mouth of Yahweh.
And the counted ones of the clans of the sons of Merari, according to their clans, according to the house of their fathers,
from a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years, everyone who comes to the army for service in the tent of meeting,
and their counted ones according to their clans were 3,200.
These were the counted ones of the clans of the sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron counted upon the mouth of Yahweh in the hand of Moses.
All the counted ones whom Moses and Aaron and the leaders of Israel counted, the Levites according to their clans and according to the house of their fathers,
from a son of 30 years and upward and up to a son of 50 years, everyone who comes to serve the service of service and the service of burden in the tent of meeting,
and their counted ones were 8,580.
Upon the mouth of Yahweh he counted them in the hand of Moses, man by man in accordance with his service and in accordance with his burden. So these were his counted ones that Yahweh commanded Moses.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Command the sons of Israel, and they shall send from the camp everyone with a skin disease and everyone flowing and everyone unclean to a person.
From male up to female you shall send, to the outside of the camp you shall send them. And they shall not make unclean their camp, where I am dwelling in the midst of them.”
And the sons of Israel did so, and they sent them to the outside of the camp. Just as Yahweh spoke to Moses, thus the sons of Israel did.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel: ‘A man or a woman, when they do from all the sins of the man to be unfaithful in unfaithfulness against Yahweh, then that person is guilty.
And they shall confess their sin that they did, and he shall return his guilt in its head and he shall add its fifth upon it, and he shall give to him to whom he is guilty.

And if there is not one redeeming for the man to return the guilt to him, the guilt being returned is to Yahweh to the priest, besides the ram of the atonement upon which he shall make atonement for him.
And every contribution of all the holy things of the sons of Israel that they present to the priest, to him it shall be.
And a man, his holy things shall be to him; a man, what he gives to the priest, to him shall be.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘A man, a man, when his wife turns aside and is unfaithful against him in unfaithfulness,

and a man lies with her the lying of seed, and it is concealed from the eyes of her husband, and it is hidden, and she, she is made unclean, but there is no witness against her, and she, she has not been caught,

and a spirit of jealousy passes over on him and he is jealous of his wife and she, she is made unclean, or a spirit of jealousy passes over on him and he is jealous of his wife and she, she is not made unclean,

then the man shall bring his wife to the priest. And he shall bring her offering upon her, the tenth of the ephah of flour of barley. He shall not pour oil on it, and he shall not put frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of memorial causing iniquity to be remembered.
And the priest shall present her, and he shall stand her to the face of Yahweh.
And the priest shall take holy water in a vessel of clay, and the priest shall take from the dust that is on the floor of the Dwelling, and he shall put it to the water.

And the priest shall stand the woman to the face of Yahweh, and he shall loose the head of the woman, and he shall put on her palm the grain offering of memorial. It is a grain offering of jealousy. And in the hand of the priest shall be the water of bitterness that brings a curse.

And the priest shall make her swear, and he shall say to the woman, “If a man did not lie with you, and if you did not turn aside to uncleanness under your husband, be free from this water of bitterness that brings a curse.
But you, if you did turn aside under your husband, and if you were made unclean and a man gave his lying in you besides your husband,”

and the priest shall make the woman swear with the oath of the curse. And the priest shall say to the woman, “Yahweh will give you to a curse and to an oath in the midst of your people when Yahweh gives your thigh falling and your belly swelling.
And this water that brings a curse will go in your insides to cause belly to swell and to cause thigh to fall.” And the woman shall say, “Amen, amen.”
And the priest shall write these curses in the book, and he shall wipe them out into the water of bitterness.
And he shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings a curse, and the water that brings a curse to bitterness will go in her.
And the priest shall take from the hand of the woman the grain offering of jealousy, and he shall raise the grain offering to the face of Yahweh, and he shall present it to the altar.
And the priest shall grab from the grain offering its memorial portion and he shall cause it to become smoke on the altar. And after he shall make the woman drink the water.

And he shall make her drink the water. And it will happen, if she was made unclean and was unfaithful in unfaithfulness against her husband, then the water that brings a curse to bitterness will go in her, and her belly will swell and her thigh will fall, and the woman will become a curse in the midst of her people.
And if the woman was not made unclean, and she is clean, then she will be free, and she will be sown with seed.
This is the law of jealousy when a woman turns aside under her husband and is made unclean,
or a man, when a spirit of jealousy passes over on him and he is jealous of his wife, then he shall stand the woman to the face of Yahweh, and the priest shall do all this law to her.
And the man will be free from iniquity, and this woman will lift up her iniquity.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘A man or a woman, when he does something wonderful to vow a vow of a Nazirite to make separate to Yahweh,

he shall make separate from wine and intoxicating drink. He shall not drink the vinegar of wine and the vinegar of intoxicating drink, and he shall not drink all the juice of grapes, and he shall not eat fresh and dried grapes.
All the days of his separation, he shall not eat from all that is made from the vine of the wine, from seeds and up to skin.

All the days of the vow of his separation, a razor shall not pass over upon his head. Until the fulfilling of the days that he made separate to Yahweh, he shall be holy and shall make great the locks of the hair of his head.
All the days of him making separate to Yahweh, he shall not go upon a person having died.
For his father and for his mother, for his brother and for his sister, he shall not make himself unclean for them in their death, because the separation of his God is upon his head.
All the days of his separation he is holy to Yahweh.

And if a dying one dies upon him in suddenness suddenly, and he makes the head of his separation unclean, then he shall shave his head on the day of his purification; on the seventh day he shall shave it.
And on the eighth day he shall bring two doves or two sons of a pigeon to the priest, to the opening of the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall make one for a sin offering and one for a burnt offering, and he shall make atonement for him from what he sinned upon the person. And he shall consecrate his head on that day.

And he shall make separate to Yahweh the days of his separation, and he shall bring a male lamb, a son of his year, for a guilt offering. And the first days shall fall, because his separation was made unclean.
And this is the law of the Nazirite: On the day of the fulfilling of the days of his separation, he shall bring him to the opening of the tent of meeting,

and he shall present his offering to Yahweh: one perfect male lamb, a son of his year, for a burnt offering; and one perfect female lamb, a daughter of her year, for a sin offering; and one perfect ram for a peace offering;
and a basket of unleavened bread, flour of loaves mixed with the oil and wafers of unleavened bread anointed with the oil; and their grain offering and their drink offerings.
And the priest shall present to the face of Yahweh, and he shall make his sin offering and his burnt offering.
And the ram he shall make a sacrifice of peace offering to Yahweh upon the basket of the unleavened bread. And the priest shall make his grain offering and his drink offering.

And the Nazirite shall shave the head of his separation at the opening of the tent of meeting, and he shall take the hair of the head of his separation, and he shall put it on the fire that is under the sacrifice of peace offering.

And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder from the ram and one loaf of unleavened bread from the basket and one wafer of unleavened bread, and he shall put them on the palms of the Nazirite after his shaving his separation.

And the priest shall raise them as a raised offering to the face of Yahweh. It is a holy thing for the priest upon the breast of the raised offering and upon the thigh of the contribution. And after, the Nazirite will drink wine.

This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to Yahweh upon his separation, besides what his hand reaches. According to the mouth of his vow that he vowed, thus he shall do in accordance with the law of his separation.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying, ‘Thus you shall bless the sons of Israel, saying to them,
“May Yahweh bless you, and may he keep you.
May Yahweh make his face shine to you, and may he be gracious to you.
May Yahweh lift up his face to you and may he put peace to you.”’
And they shall put my name on the sons of Israel, and I, I will bless them.”

And it happened on the day of Moses finishing to raise up the Dwelling that he anointed it and consecrated it and all its vessels and the altar and all its vessels. And he anointed them, and he consecrated them.
And the leaders of Israel, the heads of the houses of their fathers, presented. They were the leaders of the tribes. They were the ones standing over the counted ones.

And they brought their offering to the face of Yahweh, six covered wagons and 12 cattle, a wagon according to two of the leaders and an ox for one. And they presented them to the face of the Dwelling.
And Yahweh said to Moses, saying,
“Take from with them, and they shall be to serve the service of the tent of meeting. And you shall give them to the Levites, a man according to the mouth of his service.”
So Moses took the wagons and the cattle, and he gave them to the Levites.
Two of the wagons and four of the cattle he gave to the sons of Gershon, according to the mouth of their service,
and four of the wagons and eight of the cattle he gave to the sons of Merari, according to the mouth of their service, in the hand of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.
But to the sons of Kohath he did not give, for the service of the holy place was upon them. They carried on the shoulder.
And the leaders presented the dedication of the altar on the day of its being anointed. And the leaders presented their offering to the face of the altar.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “One leader for the day, one leader for the day, they shall present their offering for the dedication of the altar.”
And the one presenting his offering on the first day was Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, for the tribe of Judah.
And his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab.
On the second day, Nethanel, the son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, presented.
He presented his offering: one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Nethanel, the son of Zuar.
On the third day, the leader for the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, the son of Helon:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Eliab, the son of Helon.
On the fourth day, the leader for the sons of Reuben, Elizur, the son of Shedeur:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Elizur, the son of Shedeur.
On the fifth day, the leader for the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai.
On the sixth day, the leader for the sons of Gad, Eliasaph, the son of Deuel:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Eliasaph, the son of Deuel.
On the seventh day, the leader for the sons of Ephraim, Elishama, the son of Ammihud:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Elishama, the son of Ammihud.
On the eighth day, the leader for the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur.
On the ninth day, the leader for the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, the son of Gideoni:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Abidan, the son of Gideoni.
On the tenth day, the leader for the sons of Dan, Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai.
On the day of the eleventh day, the leader for the sons of Asher, Pagiel, the son of Okran:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Pagiel, the son of Okran.
On the day of the twelfth day, the leader for the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, the son of Enan:
his offering was one silver dish (its weight 130), one silver basin (70 shekels in the shekel of the holy place), the two of them full of flour mixed with the oil for a grain offering,
one palm-sized bowl (ten gold) full of incense,
one bull (a son of cattle), one ram, one male lamb (a son of his year) for a burnt offering,
one buck of the goats for a sin offering,
and for the sacrifice of the peace offering two cattle, five rams, five male goats, five male lambs (sons of a year). This was the offering of Ahira, the son of Enan.
This was the dedication of the altar on the day of its being anointed from with the leaders of Israel: 12 dishes of silver, 12 basins of silver, 12 palm-sized bowls of gold.
130 was the one silver bowl, and 70 was the one basin. All the silver of the vessels was 2, 400 in the shekel of the holy place.
The palm-sized bowls of gold were 12, full of incense. Ten, ten was the palm-sized bowl in the shekel of the holy place. All the gold of the palm-sized bowls was 120.
All the cattle for the burnt offering were 12 bulls, 12 rams, 12 male lambs (sons of a year), and their grain offering. And 12 bucks of the goats were for a sin offering.
And all the cattle of the sacrifice of the peace offering were 24 bulls, 60 rams, 60 male goats, 60 male lambs (sons of a year). This was the dedication of the altar after its being anointed.

And when Moses went to the tent of meeting to speak with him, then he heard the voice speaking to him from upon the atonement lid that is on the Box of the Testimony, from between the two cherubs. And he spoke to him.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to Aaron and say to him, ‘When you lift up the lamps, the seven lamps shall shine to the front of the face of the lampstand.’”
And Aaron did so. To the front of the face of the lampstand he lifted up its lamps, just as Yahweh commanded Moses.

And this is the work of the lampstand: a hammered work of gold; it was a hammered work up to its side, up to its blossom. According to the appearance that Yahweh showed Moses, so he made the lampstand.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Take the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel and cleanse them.

And thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: Sprinkle on them the water of the sin offering. And they shall pass a razor over all their flesh, and they shall wash their clothes, and they shall cleanse themselves.
And they shall take a bull, a son of cattle, and its grain offering of flour mixed with the oil. And you shall take a second bull, a son of cattle, for a sin offering.
And you shall present the Levites to the face of the tent of meeting. And you shall assemble all the congregation of the sons of Israel.
And you shall present the Levites to the face of Yahweh, and the sons of Israel shall lay their hands on the Levites.
And Aaron shall raise the Levites as a raised offering to the face of Yahweh from with the sons of Israel. And they shall be to serve the service of Yahweh.
And the Levites shall lay their hands on the head of the bulls, and make the one a sin offering and the one a burnt offering to Yahweh, to make atonement for the Levites.
And you shall stand the Levites to the face of Aaron and to the face of his sons, and you shall raise them as a raised offering to Yahweh.
And you shall separate the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel, and the Levites shall be for me.
And after that the Levites shall go to serve the tent of meeting, and you shall cleanse them and you shall raise them as a raised offering.
For they are given, given to me from the midst of the sons of Israel. Instead of the opener of every womb, the firstborn of all from the sons of Israel, I have taken them for me.
For to me is every firstborn among the sons of Israel, among the man and among the animal. On the day of my striking down every firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated them to me.
And I took the Levites instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel.

And I gave the Levites as given ones to Aaron and to his sons from the midst of the sons of Israel, to serve the service of the sons of Israel in the tent of meeting and to make atonement for the sons of Israel. And a plague will not be among the sons of Israel when the sons of Israel come near to the holy place.”
And Moses, and Aaron and all the congregation of the sons of Israel, did to the Levites according to all that Yahweh had commanded Moses for the Levites; thus the sons of Israel did to them.
And the Levites purified themselves, and they washed their clothes, and Aaron raised them as a raised offering to the face of Yahweh, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.

And after that, the Levites went to serve their service in the tent of meeting to the face of Aaron and to the face of his sons. Just as Yahweh had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did to them.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“This is what is for the Levites: From a son of 25 years and upward he shall come to fight in the army in the service of the tent of meeting.
And from a son of 50 years he shall return from the army of the service, and he shall not serve more.
And he shall minister to his brothers in the tent of meeting to keep keeping, but he shall not serve service. Thus you shall do for the Levites in their keepings.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the second year of their going out from the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying,
“And the sons of Israel shall do the Passover in its appointed time.
On day 14 in this month, between the evenings, you shall do it in its appointed time. According to all its statutes and according to all its ordinances you shall do it.”
And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel to do the Passover.
And they did the Passover in the first, on day 14 of the month, between the evenings, in the wilderness of Sinai. According to all that Yahweh had commanded Moses, so the sons of Israel did.
And there were men who were unclean to a person of a man, and they were not able to do the Passover on that day. And they came near to the face of Moses and to the face of Aaron on that day.
And those men said to him, “We are unclean to a person of a man. Why are we taken away not to present the offering of Yahweh in its appointed time in the midst of the sons of Israel?”
And Moses said to them, “Stand, and I shall hear what Yahweh commands for you.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel, saying, ‘A man, a man, when he is unclean to a person or is on a distant way, for you or for your generations, then he will do the Passover to Yahweh.
In the second month on day 14, between the evenings, they shall do it. With unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.
They shall not leave from it until morning, and a bone they shall not break in it. According to every statute of the Passover they shall do it.

And the man who is clean and is not on a way and ceases to do the Passover, then that person shall be cut off from his people, for he did not present the offering of Yahweh in its appointed time. That man shall lift up his sin.

And if a sojourner sojourns with you and does the Passover to Yahweh according to the statute of the Passover and according to its ordinance, so he shall do. One statute shall be for you and for the sojourner and for the native of the land.’”
And on the day of the raising up of the Dwelling, the cloud covered the Dwelling of the Tent of the Testimony, and in the evening it is upon the Dwelling like the appearance of fire until morning.
So it is continually; the cloud covers it, and the appearance of fire is at night.
And to the mouth of the cloud being taken up from upon the tent, and after that the sons of Israel set out. And in a place where the cloud dwells there, there the sons of Israel camp.
Upon the mouth of Yahweh the sons of Israel set out, and upon the mouth of Yahweh they camp. All the days that the cloud dwells upon the Dwelling they camp.
And when the cloud prolongs upon the Dwelling many days, then the sons of Israel keep the keeping of Yahweh, and they do not set out.
And it is that the cloud is days of number upon the Dwelling, upon the mouth of Yahweh they camp, and upon the mouth of Yahweh they set out.
And it is that the cloud is from evening until morning, and the cloud is taken up in the morning, then they set out; or during the day and at night, and the cloud is taken up, then they set out.
Whether two days or a month or days, when the cloud prolongs upon the Dwelling to dwell upon it, the sons of Israel camp and do not set out. And when it is taken up, they set out.
Upon the mouth of Yahweh they camp, and upon the mouth of Yahweh they set out. They kept the keeping of Yahweh upon the mouth of Yahweh in the hand of Moses.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Make for you two trumpets of silver. You shall make them hammered work, and they shall be for you for the assembly of the congregation and for the departure of the camps,
and they shall blow them, and all the congregation shall congregate to you to the opening of the tent of meeting.
And if they blow one, then the leaders, the heads of the thousands of Israel, shall congregate to you.
And you shall blow an alarm, and the camps camping to the east shall set out,
and you shall blow a second alarm, and the camps camping to the south shall set out. They shall blow an alarm for their setting out.
And when assembling the assembly, you shall blow but you shall not blow an alarm.
And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets, and they shall be for you for a statute of eternity, for your generations.

And when you go to a battle in your land against the opponent who opposes you, then you shall blow an alarm on the trumpets, and you shall be remembered to the face of Yahweh your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.

And on the day of your joy and at your appointed times and at the heads of your months, then you shall blow the trumpets upon your burnt offerings and upon the sacrifices of your peace offerings, and they will be for you for a memorial to the face of your God. I am Yahweh your God.”
And it happened in the second year, in the second month, on 20 in the month, the cloud was taken up from upon the Dwelling of the Testimony.
And the sons of Israel set out according to their departures from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud dwelled in the wilderness of Paran.
And they set out in the first upon the mouth of Yahweh in the hand of Moses.
And the banner of the camp of the sons of Judah set out in the first according to their armies: and over his army was Nahshon, the son of Amminadab,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Issachar was Nethanel, the son of Zuar,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun was Eliab, the son of Helon.
And the Dwelling was taken down, and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari, the ones carrying the Dwelling, set out.
And the banner of the camp of Reuben set out according to their armies: and over his army was Elizur, the son of Shedeur,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Simeon was Shelumiel, the son of Zurishaddai,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Gad was Eliasaph, the son of Deuel.
And the Kohathites, the ones carrying the holy place, set out, and they raised up the Dwelling until their coming.
And the banner of the camp of the sons of Ephraim set out according to their armies: and over his army was Elishama, the son of Ammihud,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh was Gamaliel, the son of Pedahzur,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin was Abidan, the son of Gideoni.
And the banner of the camp of the sons of Dan, the one gathering for all the camps, set out according to their armies: and over his army was Ahiezer, the son of Ammishaddai.
And over the army of the tribe of the sons of Asher was Pagiel, the son of Okran,
and over the army of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali was Ahira, the son of Enan.
These were the departures of the sons of Israel according to their armies. And they set out.

And Moses said to Hobab, the son of Reuel, the Midianite, the father-in-law of Moses, “We are setting out to the place that Yahweh said, ‘I shall give it to you.’ Go with us, and we will do good to you. For Yahweh has spoken good upon Israel.”
And he said to him, “I will not go, except I will go to my land and to my relatives.”
And he said, “Please do not leave us! For upon this, you know our camping in the wilderness, and you will be for us for eyes.
And it shall be that when you go with us, then it will happen that that good that Yahweh shall do good with us, also we shall do good to you.”
And they set out from the mountain of Yahweh a way of three days, and the box of the covenant of Yahweh was setting out to their face a way of three days, to seek out for them rest,
and the cloud of Yahweh was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
And it happened, when the box set out, that Moses said, “Rise up, Yahweh, and your enemies will scatter, and the ones hating you will flee from your face.”
And when it rested, he says, “Return, Yahweh, the ten thousands of the thousands of Israel.”

And it happened that the people were like those complaining of evil in the ears of Yahweh, and Yahweh heard, and his nose burned, and the fire of Yahweh burned among them, and it ate at the end of the camp.
And the people cried out to Moses, and Moses prayed to Yahweh, and the fire sank;
and he called the name of that place Taberah, for the fire of Yahweh burned among them.
And the rabble that was in its midst desired a desire, and also the sons of Israel returned, and they wept. And they said, “Who will feed us flesh?
We remember the fish that we ate in Egypt for nothing, the cucumbers, and the watermelons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlic.
And now our life is dry; there is not anything except for the manna in our eyes.”
And the manna, it was like the seed of coriander, and its eye was like the eye of bdellium.

The people went about, and they gathered, and they ground in the mill or crushed in the mortar, and they boiled in the pot, and they made it cakes, and its taste was like the taste of the freshness of the oil.
And when the dew came down on the camp at night, the manna came down upon it.
And Moses heard the people weeping according to its clans, a man to the opening of his tent; and the nose of Yahweh burned exceedingly, and in the eyes of Moses it was evil.
And Moses said to Yahweh, “Why do you do evil to your servant? And why have I not found favor in your eyes, to put the burden of all this people on me?
Did I, I conceive all this people? Or did I, I bear him, that you say to me, ‘Carry him in your bosom just as the one nursing carries the one sucking,’ over the ground that you swore to his fathers?
From where is flesh for me to give to all this people? For they weep upon me, saying, ‘Give to us flesh and let us eat.’
I, I am not able by myself to carry all this people, for it is heavy from me.
And if you are doing thus to me, killing, please kill me, if I have found favor in your eyes, and let me not look on my evil.”

And Yahweh said to Moses, “Gather for me 70 men from the elders of Israel whom you know that they are elders of the people and its officers, and you shall take them to the tent of meeting, and they shall station themselves there with you.

And I will come down and speak with you there, and I will take away from the Spirit that is on you, and I will put on them, and they will carry the burden of the people with you, and you, you will not carry by yourself.

And to the people you shall say, ‘Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat flesh. For you wept in the ears of Yahweh, saying, “Who will feed us flesh? For it was good for us in Egypt.” And Yahweh shall give to you flesh, and you shall eat.
You shall eat not one day and not two days and not five days and not ten days and not 20 days,

until a month of days, until when it goes out from your nose. And it shall be for nausea for you because you rejected Yahweh who is in your midst, and you wept to his face, saying, “Why is this: we went out from Egypt?”’”
And Moses said, “The people in whose midst I am are 600, 000 on foot, but you, you said, ‘I shall give flesh to them, and they shall eat a month of days.’
Shall flock and cattle be slaughtered for them, and it shall find for them? Or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered for them, and it shall find for them?”
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Is the hand of Yahweh short? Now you will see whether my word will happen to you or not.”
And Moses went out, and he spoke to the people the words of Yahweh. And he gathered 70 men from the elders of the people, and he stood them around the tent.

And Yahweh came down in the cloud, and he spoke to him, and he took away from the Spirit that was on him, and he gave on 70 men, the elders. And it happened, as the Spirit rested on them, that they prophesied, and they did not add.

And two of the men were left in the camp: The name of the one was Eldad, and the name of the second was Medad. And the Spirit rested on them, and they were among the written ones, but they did not go out to the tent; and they prophesied in the camp.
And the young man ran, and he told to Moses, and he said, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp.”
And Joshua, the son of Nun, the one ministering to Moses from his youth, answered and said, “My lord Moses, restrain them.”
And Moses said to him, “Are you being jealous for me? And who will give all the people of Yahweh as prophets, that Yahweh will give his Spirit on them?”
And Moses was gathered to the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

And a wind set out from with Yahweh, and it brought quails from the sea, and it left upon the camp as a way of a day this way and as a way of a day that way around the camp and about two cubits on the face of the land.

And the people rose all that day and all the night and all the day of the next day, and they gathered the quail. The one making little gathered ten homers. And spreading, they spread for them around the camp.
The flesh was still between their teeth, before it was cut off, and the nose of Yahweh burned against the people, and Yahweh struck the people an exceedingly great striking,
and he called the name of that place Kibroth Hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had desired.
From Kibroth Hattaavah the people set out to Hazeroth. And they were in Hazeroth.
And Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses on account of the woman, the Cushitess, that he took. For he took a woman, a Cushitess.
And they said, “Did Yahweh really only speak in Moses? Did he not speak also in us?” And Yahweh heard.
(And the man Moses was very meek, more than the man who was upon the face of the ground.)
And Yahweh said suddenly to Moses and to Aaron and to Miriam, “Go out, the three of you, to the tent of meeting.” And the three of them went out.
And Yahweh came down in a pillar of cloud, and it stood at the opening of the tent, and he called Aaron and Miriam, and the two of them went out.
And he said, “Please hear my words! If there is a prophet of yours of Yahweh, in the vision I will make myself known to him. In the dream I will speak with him.
Not so my servant Moses; in all my house he, he is faithful.
Mouth to mouth I speak with him, and appearance and not in riddles, and he looks at the form of Yahweh. And why do you not fear to speak against my servant, against Moses?”
And the nose of Yahweh burned against them, and he went,
and the cloud turned aside from upon the tent. And behold, Miriam was with a skin disease, like the snow, and Aaron turned to Miriam, and behold, she was with a skin disease,
and Aaron said to Moses, “Please, my lord, please do not put upon us sin which we did foolishly and which we sinned.
May she not be like the one having died, who, when he comes out from the womb of his mother, then half of his flesh was eaten.”
And Moses cried out to Yahweh, saying, “God, please, please heal her!”

And Yahweh said to Moses, “And her father, spitting, spit in her face, would she not be humiliated seven days? She shall be shut up seven days from outside to the camp, and after she shall be gathered.”
And Miriam was shut up from outside to the camp seven days, and the people did not set out until Miriam was gathered.
And after, the people set out from Hazeroth, and they camped in the wilderness of Paran.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Send for you men, and they shall explore the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the sons of Israel. One man, one man to the tribe of his fathers you shall send, every one a leader among them.”
And Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran upon the mouth of Yahweh. All of them were men; they were heads of the sons of Israel.
And these were their names: for the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, the son of Zaccur;
for the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, the son of Hori;
for the tribe of Judah, Caleb, the son of Jephunneh;
for the tribe of Issachar, Igal, the son of Joseph;
for the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, the son of Nun;
for the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, the son of Raphu;
for the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, the son of Sodi;
for the tribe of Joseph, for the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi, the son of Susi;
for the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, the son of Gemalli;
for the tribe of Asher, Sethur, the son of Michael;
for the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, the son of Vophsi;
for the tribe of Gad, Geuel, the son of Maki.
These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to explore the land; and Moses called Hoshea, the son of Nun, Joshua.
And Moses sent them to explore the land of Canaan, and he said to them, “Go up this in the Negev, and you shall go up the hill country.
And you shall see the land, what it is; and the people dwelling upon it, whether it is strong or weak, whether it is few or many;
and what is the land in which it is dwelling, whether it is good or evil; and what are the cities in which it is dwelling, whether in camps or in fortresses;

and what is the land, whether it is fat or lean, whether there are trees in it or not, and you shall strengthen yourselves, and you shall take from the fruit of the land.” (And the days were the days of the firstfruits of the grapes.)
And they went up, and they explored the land from the wilderness of Zin up to Rehob, Lebo Hamath.
And they went up in the Negev, and they came as far as Hebron. And there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, those born of the Anak. (And Hebron was built seven years to the face of Zoan, Egypt.)
And they came as far as Wadi Eshkol, and they cut from there a branch and one cluster of grapes, and they carried it on the carrying pole with two, and from the pomegranates and from the figs.
He called that place Wadi Eshkol on account of the cluster that the sons of Israel cut from there.
And they returned from exploring the land at the end of 40 days,

and they went and they came to Moses and to Aaron and to all the congregation of the sons of Israel, to the wilderness of Paran at Kadesh. And they returned them and all the congregation a word, and they showed them the fruit of the land,
and they recounted to him, and they said, “We went to the land where you sent us, and indeed it is flowing with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
However, the people dwelling in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified very great, and also we saw those born of the Anak there.

Amalek is dwelling in the land of the Negev, and the Hittite and the Jebusite and the Amorite is dwelling in the hill country, and the Canaanite is dwelling upon the sea and upon the hand of the Jordan.”
And Caleb hushed the people to Moses, and he said, “Going up, we will go up, and we will possess it, for being able, we are able against it.”
But the men who went up with him said, “We are not able to go up against the people, for it is stronger than us.”

And they brought out a bad report of the land that they explored to the sons of Israel, saying, “The land through which we passed to explore it, it is a land eating the ones dwelling in it. And all the people whom we saw in the midst of it were men of size,
and there we saw the Nephils (the sons of Anak are from the Nephils), and we were in our eyes like the grasshoppers, and thus we were in their eyes.”
And all the congregation lifted up, and they gave their voice, and the people wept in that night.
And all the sons of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron. And all the congregation said to them, “If only we died in the land of Egypt, or if only we died in this wilderness!
And why is Yahweh bringing us to this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and our children will be for plunder. Is it not good for us to return to Egypt?”
And they said, a man to his brother, “We shall give a head, and we shall return to Egypt.”
And Moses fell and Aaron on their faces to the face of all the assembly of the congregation of the sons of Israel,
and Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, from the ones exploring the land, tore their clothes,
and they said to all the congregation of the sons of Israel, saying, “The land through which we passed to explore it is a very, very good land.
If Yahweh delights in us, then he will bring us to this land, and he will give it to us, a land that is flowing with milk and honey.

Only you shall not rebel against Yahweh, and you, you shall not fear the people of the land, for they are our bread. Their shadow turned aside from upon them, and Yahweh is with us. You shall not fear them!”
And all the congregation said to stone them with stones. But the glory of Yahweh appeared in the tent of meeting to all the sons of Israel.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Until when will this people spurn me? And until when will it not believe in me, in all the signs that I have done in its midst?
I will strike it with the plague, and I will dispossess it, and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than it.”
And Moses said to Yahweh, “And Egypt shall hear, for you brought this people up with your power from its midst,

and they shall say to the one dwelling in this land. They have heard that you, Yahweh, are in the midst of this people, who appears eye in eye, you, Yahweh, and your cloud is standing over them, and in a pillar of cloud you are going before to their face by day and in a pillar of fire at night.
And you kill this people as one man, then the nations who heard your hearing will say, saying,
‘From Yahweh not being able to bring this people to the land that he swore to them, so he slaughtered them in the wilderness.’
And now, please may the power of my Lord be great, just as you spoke, saying,

‘Yahweh is long of nostrils and great of covenant faithfulness, lifting up iniquity and transgression. But leaving unpunished, he will not leave unpunished, visiting iniquity of fathers upon sons, upon thirds, and upon fourths.’
Please forgive this people for the iniquity according to the greatness of your covenant faithfulness and just as you lifted up for this people from Egypt and as far as here.”
And Yahweh said, “I have forgiven according to your word.
However, as I live and as all the land is filled with the glory of Yahweh,
that all the men seeing my glory and my signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and they tested me this ten times, and they did not hear my voice,
if they see the land that I swore to their fathers. And all the ones spurning me will not see it.
But my servant Caleb, because another spirit was with him, and he filled after me, so I will bring him to the land where he went, and his seed will possess it.
And the Amalekite and the Canaanite are dwelling in the valley. Tomorrow turn and set out for you to the wilderness, the way of the Sea of Reeds.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
“Until when to this evil congregation, that they are murmuring against me? I have heard the murmurings of the sons of Israel, which they are murmuring against me.
Say to them, ‘As I live—the declaration of Yahweh—if not just as you spoke in my ears, thus I will do to you:
In this wilderness your corpses will fall and all your counted ones according to all your number, from a son of 20 years and upward, who murmured against me;
if you go to the land that I have lifted up my hand to make you dwell in it, except Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, the son of Nun,
and your children whom you said will be for plunder. And I will bring them, and they will know the land that you rejected.
And you, your corpses will fall in this wilderness,
and your sons will be shepherds in the wilderness 40 years, and they will lift up your fornications until the finishing of your corpses in the wilderness.
By the number of the days that you explored the land, 40 days, a day for the year, a day for the year, you will lift up your iniquities 40 years. And you will know my opposition.’
I, Yahweh, have spoken. If I do not do this to all this evil congregation, the ones congregating against me. In this wilderness they will finish, and there they will die.”
And the men whom Moses sent to explore the land and returned and made all the congregation murmur against him by bringing out a bad report against the land,
and they died, the men bringing out an evil bad report of the land, in the plague to the face of Yahweh.
But Joshua, the son of Nun, and Caleb, the son of Jephunneh, lived from those men, the ones having gone to explore the land.
And Moses spoke these words to all the sons of Israel, and the people mourned exceedingly,
and they rose early in the morning and went up to the head of the hill country, saying, “Behold us! And we will go up to the place that Yahweh said, for we sinned.”
And Moses said, “Why is this—are you passing over the mouth of Yahweh? And it will not succeed.
You shall not go up, for Yahweh is not in your midst, and you shall not be struck to the face of your enemies.
For the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there to your face, and you will fall by the sword, for upon this, you returned from after Yahweh, and Yahweh will not be with you.”
And they presumed to go up to the head of the hill country, but the box of the covenant of Yahweh and Moses did not depart from the midst of the camp.
And the Amalekite and the Canaanite, the one dwelling in that hill country, came down. And they struck them and beat them to pieces as far as the Hormah.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘When you go to the land of your dwellings, which I am giving to you,

and you make a gift to Yahweh, a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to make extraordinary a vow, or in a freewill offering, or at your appointed times, to make a scent of pleasing for Yahweh from the cattle or from the flock,
then the one presenting shall present his offering to Yahweh, a grain offering, a tenth of flour mixed with a fourth of the hin of oil.
And wine for the drink offering, a fourth of the hin, you shall make upon the burnt offering or for the sacrifice, for the one male lamb.
Or for the ram you shall make a grain offering, two tenths of flour mixed with a third of the hin of the oil,
and wine for the drink offering, a third of the hin. You shall present a scent of pleasing to Yahweh.
And when you make a son of cattle a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to make extraordinary a vow or a peace offering to Yahweh,
then he shall present upon the son of cattle a grain offering, three tenths of flour mixed with half of the hin of the oil,
and you shall present for the drink offering half of the hin of wine; a gift, a scent of pleasing for Yahweh.
Thus it shall be done for the one ox or for the one ram or for the small livestock among the male lambs or among the goats.
According to the number that you do, thus you shall do for the one according to their number.
Every native shall do these thus to present a gift, a scent of pleasing for Yahweh.
And if a sojourner sojourns with you, or who is in your midst for your generations, and he makes a gift, a scent of pleasing for Yahweh, just as you shall do, thus he shall do.
For the assembly, a statute is one for you and for the sojourner, the one sojourning, a statute of eternity for your generations. As you, so the sojourner shall be to the face of Yahweh.
One law and one ordinance shall be for you and for the sojourner, the one sojourning with you.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘When you go to the land where I am bringing you,
and it shall be, when you eat from the bread of the land, you shall raise a contribution to Yahweh.
The first of your dough, a loaf, you shall raise as a contribution. As a contribution of the threshing floor, so you shall raise it.
From the first of your dough you shall give a contribution to Yahweh for your generations.
And when you err and do not do all these commandments that Yahweh has spoken to Moses,
all that Yahweh has commanded to you in the hand of Moses, from the day that Yahweh commanded and beyond for your generations,

then it shall be, if from the eyes of the congregation it was done by mistake, that all the congregation shall make a bull (one son of cattle) for a burnt offering, for a scent of pleasing for Yahweh, and its grain offering and its drink offering, according to the ordinance, and one buck of the goats for a sin offering.

And the priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the sons of Israel, and it shall be forgiven for them, for it was a mistake, and they, they brought their offering, a gift, to Yahweh, and their sin offering to the face of Yahweh according to their mistake.
And it shall be forgiven for all the congregation of the sons of Israel and for the sojourner, the one sojourning in their midst, for it was to all the people by mistake.
And if one person sins by mistake, then he shall present a female goat, a daughter of her year, for a sin offering.
And the priest shall make atonement for the person, the one erring in a sin by mistake to the face of Yahweh, to make atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven for him.
The native among the sons of Israel and for the sojourner, the one sojourning in their midst, one law shall be for you for the one doing by mistake.
But the person who does with a hand being high, from the native and from the sojourner, he is blaspheming Yahweh, and that person shall be cut off from the midst of his people,
for he despised the word of Yahweh and broke his commandment. Being cut off, that person shall be cut off. His iniquity is on him.’”
And the sons of Israel were in the wilderness, and they found a man gathering wood on the day of the Sabbath,
and the ones finding him gathering wood presented him to Moses and to Aaron and to all the congregation.
And they made him rest in custody, for it had not been declared what should be done to him.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Dying, the man shall be put to death, all the congregation stoning him with the stones from outside to the camp.”
And all the congregation brought him out to from outside to the camp, and they stoned him with the stones, and he died, just as Yahweh commanded Moses.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘And they shall make for themselves a tassel on the hems of their clothes for their generations, and they shall put on the tassel of the hem a cord of blue,

and it shall be for you for a tassel, and you shall see it, and you shall remember all the commandments of Yahweh, and you shall do them, and you shall not explore after your heart and after your eyes, after which you are committing fornication,
so that you shall remember and do all my commandments, and you shall be holy for your God.
I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out from the land of Egypt to be for you for a God. I am Yahweh your God.’”
And Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, took, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, the sons of Reuben,
and they rose to the face of Moses, and 250 men from the sons of Israel, leaders of the congregation, called ones of meeting, men of name,

and they assembled against Moses and against Aaron. And they said to them, “Great to you! For all of the congregation, all of them, are holy, and Yahweh is in their midst. And why do you lift yourselves up above the assembly of Yahweh?”
And Moses heard and fell on his face.
And he spoke to Korah and to all his congregation, saying, “Morning, and Yahweh will make known who is to him and the holy one, and he will present to him, and whom he chooses he will present to him.
Do this: take for you censers, Korah and all his congregation,
and put fire in them, and set incense on them to the face of Yahweh tomorrow. And it will happen that the man whom Yahweh chooses, he is the holy one. Great to you, sons of Levi!”
And Moses said to Korah, “Please hear, sons of Levi:

Is it small from you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel to present you to him to serve the service of the Dwelling of Yahweh and to stand to the face of the congregation to minister to them?
And he has presented you and all your brothers, the sons of Levi, with you, but do you seek the priesthood also?
Therefore, you and all your congregation are the ones congregating against Yahweh, and Aaron, what is he that you murmur against him?”
And Moses sent to call for Dathan and for Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and they said, “We will not go up.
Is it small that you have brought us up from a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness that you make yourself rule over us, also making yourself rule?
Also, you have not brought us to a land flowing with milk and honey, or given to us an inheritance of field and vineyard. Will you gouge out the eyes of these men? We will not go up.”
And it burned exceedingly for Moses, and he said to Yahweh, “Do not turn to their grain offering. I have not lifted up one donkey from them. And I have not done evil to one from them.”
And Moses said to Korah, “You and all your congregation, be to the face of Yahweh, you and they and Aaron, tomorrow.
And take a man his censer, and put incense on them, and present to the face of Yahweh, a man his censer, 250 censers, and you and Aaron, a man his censer.”
And they took a man his censer, and they put fire on them and set incense on them, and they stood at the opening of the tent of meeting, and Moses and Aaron.
And Korah assembled all the congregation against them to the opening of the tent of meeting, and the glory of Yahweh appeared to all the congregation.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
“Separate yourselves from the midst of this congregation, and I shall finish them according to a moment.”
