And they fell on their faces and said, “God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, will one man sin and you be angry against all the congregation?”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Speak to the congregation, saying, ‘Be taken up from around to the dwelling of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.’”
And Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram, and the elders of Israel went after him.
And he spoke to the congregation, saying, “Turn aside, please, from upon the tents of these wicked men, and do not touch all that is to them, lest you be swept away in all their sins.”

And they were taken up from upon the dwelling of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram, from around, and Dathan and Abiram went out, standing at the opening of their tents, and their wives and their sons and their children.
And Moses said, “By this you will know that Yahweh has sent me to do all these works, that they are not from my heart:
If as the death of every man these die, and the oversight of every man is counted upon them, Yahweh did not send me.
But if Yahweh creates a creation, and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them and all that is to them, and they go down living to Sheol, then you will know that these men spurned Yahweh.”
And it happened, when he finished to speak all these words, that the ground that was under them was split open,
and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their houses and every man who was to Korah and all the property,
and they went down, they and all that was to them, living to Sheol, and the earth covered over them, and they perished from the midst of the assembly.
And all Israel who was around them fled to their voice, for they said, “Lest the earth swallow us!”
And fire went out from with Yahweh, and it ate the 250 men presenting the incense.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Say to Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, and he shall raise the censers from between the burning, and scatter the fire beyond, for they are holy.

The censers of these ones sinning against their lives, and they shall make them hammered sheets of thin plates, plating for the altar, for they presented them to the face of Yahweh, and they are holy, and they shall be for a sign for the sons of Israel.”
And Eleazar the priest took the censers of bronze that the ones being burned had presented, and they hammered them as plating for the altar,

a memorial for the sons of Israel, so that a strange man, who is not from the seed of Aaron, shall not come near to cause incense to become smoke to the face of Yahweh, and he shall not be like Korah and like his congregation, just as Yahweh spoke in the hand of Moses to him.
And all the congregation of the sons of Israel murmured from the next day against Moses and against Aaron, saying, “You, you killed the people of Yahweh!”
And it happened, when the congregation assembled against Moses and against Aaron, that they turned to the tent of meeting, and behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of Yahweh appeared.
And Moses and Aaron went to the face of the tent of meeting,
and Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Lift yourselves up from the midst of this congregation, and I shall finish them according to a moment.” And they fell on their faces.

And Moses said to Aaron, “Take the censer, and put fire on it from upon the altar, and set incense, and bring it quickly to the congregation, and make atonement for them, for the wrath has gone out from to the face of Yahweh. The plague has begun.”
And Aaron took, just as Moses spoke, and he ran to the midst of the assembly. And behold, the plague had begun among the people. And he put the incense, and he made atonement for the people.
And he stood between the ones dying and between the ones living. And the plague was restrained.
And the ones dying by the plague were 14, 700, besides the ones dying on account of the matter of Korah.
And Aaron returned to Moses, to the opening of the tent of meeting, and the plague was restrained.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“Speak to the sons of Israel, and take from with them a staff, a staff for a house of a father, from with all their leaders for the house of their fathers, 12 staffs. A man, his name you shall write on his staff.
And the name of Aaron you shall write on the staff of Levi, for one staff is for the head of the house of their fathers.
And you shall make them rest in the tent of meeting to the face of the Testimony, where I congregate with you there.
And it will be that the man whom I choose, his staff will sprout. And I will cause to subside from upon me the murmurings of the sons of Israel, which they are murmuring against you.”

And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel, and all their leaders gave to him a staff for one leader, a staff for one leader, for the house of their fathers, 12 staffs. And the staff of Aaron was in the midst of their staffs.
And Moses made the staffs rest to the face of Yahweh in the tent of the Testimony.

And it happened from the next day that Moses came to the tent of the Testimony, and behold, the staff of Aaron for the house of Levi had sprouted. And it brought out a blossom and it blossomed a flower, and it ripened almonds.
And Moses brought out all the staffs from to the face of Yahweh to all the sons of Israel. And they saw, and they took, a man his staff.

And Yahweh said to Moses, “Return the staff of Aaron to the face of the Testimony for keeping, for a sign for the sons of rebellion. And you shall finish their murmurings from upon me, and they shall not die.”
And Moses did. Just as Yahweh had commanded him, thus he did.
And the sons of Israel said to Moses, saying, “Behold, we expire! We perish; all of us perish!
Everyone coming near, the one coming near to the Dwelling of Yahweh, will die. Shall we finish to expire?”

And Yahweh said to Aaron, “You and your sons and the house of your father with you shall lift up the iniquity of the holy place, and you and your sons with you shall lift up the iniquity of your priesthood.

And also bring near with you your brothers, the tribe of Levi, the tribe of your father, and they shall be joined to you and shall minister to you. And you and your sons with you shall be to the face of the tent of the Testimony.
And they shall keep your keeping and the keeping of all the tent. Only to the vessels of the holy place and to the altar they shall not come near, so they will not die, both they and you.
And they shall be joined to you, and they shall keep the keeping of the tent of meeting for all the service of the tent. And a stranger shall not come near to you.
And you shall keep the keeping of the holy place and the keeping of the altar, and there shall not be again wrath against the sons of Israel.
And I, behold, I have taken your brothers the Levites from the midst of the sons of Israel, a gift for you, given to Yahweh, to serve the service of the tent of meeting.

And you and your sons with you shall keep your priesthood for every matter of the altar and for inside the veil, and you shall serve. I give your priesthood as a service of a gift. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.”

And Yahweh spoke to Aaron, “And I, behold, I have given to you the keeping of my contributions, for all the holy things of the sons of Israel. To you I have given them for a portion and to your sons for a statute of eternity.

This shall be for you from the holy thing of the holy things, from the fire: every offering of theirs, for every grain offering of theirs and for every sin offering of theirs and for every guilt offering of theirs which they return to me. It is a holy thing of holy things for you and for your sons.
In the holy of holies you shall eat it. Every male shall eat it. It shall be holy for you.

And this is for you: the contribution of their gift, for all the raised offerings of the sons of Israel. To you I have given them and to your sons and to your daughters with you for a statute of eternity. Every clean one in your house shall eat it.
All the fat of the oil and all the fat of the new wine and grain, their firstfruits which they give to Yahweh, to you I have given them.
The firstfruits of all that is in their land which they bring to Yahweh shall be for you. Every clean one in your house shall eat it.
Every devoted thing in Israel shall be for you.

Every opener of the womb for all flesh which they present to Yahweh, among the man and among the animal, shall be for you. Only redeeming you shall redeem the firstborn of the man, and the firstborn of the unclean animal you shall redeem.
And his redeemed ones from a son of a month you shall redeem by your valuation, silver of five shekels, by the shekel of the holy place (it is 20 gerahs).

Only a firstborn of an ox or a firstborn of a sheep or a firstborn of a goat you shall not redeem; they are holy. You shall splash their blood on the altar, and you shall cause their fat to become smoke, a gift for a scent of pleasing to Yahweh.
And their flesh shall be for you; like the breast of the raised offering and like the right thigh, it shall be for you.

All the contributions of the holy things that the sons of Israel raise to Yahweh I have given to you and to your sons and to your daughters with you for a statute of eternity. It is a covenant of salt of eternity to the face of Yahweh, for you and for your seed with you.”
And Yahweh said to Aaron, “In their land you shall not inherit, and a portion shall not be for you in their midst. I am your portion and your inheritance in the midst of the sons of Israel.
And to the sons of Levi, behold, I have given every tithe in Israel for an inheritance, in exchange for their service which they are serving, the service of the tent of meeting.
And the sons of Israel shall not again come near to the tent of meeting to lift up sin, to die.

And the Levite, he shall serve the service of the tent of meeting, and they shall bear their iniquity. It is a statute of eternity for your generations, and in the midst of the sons of Israel they shall not inherit an inheritance.

For the tithe of the sons of Israel, which they raise to Yahweh as a contribution, I have given to the Levites for an inheritance. Therefore I have said to them that in the midst of the sons of Israel they shall not inherit an inheritance.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“And to the Levites you shall speak, and you shall say to them, ‘When you take from with the sons of Israel the tithe which I have given to you from with them for your inheritance, then you shall raise from it a contribution of Yahweh, a tithe from the tithe.
And your contribution shall be reckoned to you as the grain from the threshing floor and as the fullness from the winepress.
Thus you shall raise, you also, the contribution of Yahweh from all your tithes which you take from with the sons of Israel, and you shall give from it the contribution of Yahweh to Aaron the priest.
From all your gifts you shall raise every contribution of Yahweh, from all its fat, its holy thing from it.’
And you shall say to them, ‘When you lift up its fat from it, then it shall be reckoned to the Levites like produce of a threshing floor and like produce of a winepress.
And you shall eat it in every place, you and your house, for it is a wage for you in exchange for your service in the tent of meeting.
And you shall not lift up sin upon it when you raise its fat from it. And the holy things of the sons of Israel you shall not profane, and you shall not die.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

“This is the statute of the law that Yahweh has commanded, saying, ‘Speak to the sons of Israel, and they shall take to you a perfect red cow in which there is no blemish, on which a yoke has not gone up.
And you shall give it to Eleazar the priest, and he shall bring it out to the outside of the camp, and he shall slaughter it to his face.
And Eleazar the priest shall take from its blood with his finger, and he shall sprinkle from its blood to the front of the face of the tent of meeting seven times.
And he shall burn the cow to his eyes; its skin and its flesh and its blood, with its dung, he shall burn.
And the priest shall take wood of cedar and hyssop and scarlet of worm, and he shall throw to the midst of the burning of the cow.
And the priest shall wash his clothes, and he shall bathe his flesh in the water, and after he shall come to the camp. And the priest shall be unclean until the evening.
And the one burning it shall wash his clothes in the water, and he shall bathe his flesh in the water, and he shall be unclean until the evening.

And a clean man shall gather the ashes of the cow and make them rest from outside to the camp in a clean place. And it shall be for the congregation of the sons of Israel for keeping, for water of impurity. It is a sin offering.

And the one gathering the ashes of the cow shall wash his clothes, and he shall be unclean until the evening. And it shall be for the sons of Israel and for the sojourner, the one sojourning in their midst, for a statute of eternity.
The one touching a dying one for every person of a man shall be unclean seven days.
He shall purify himself with it on the third day and on the seventh day. He will be clean. And if he does not purify himself on the third day and on the seventh day, he will not be clean.

Every one touching a dying one in the person of the man that dies, and he does not purify himself, he has made the Dwelling of Yahweh unclean. And that person shall be cut off from Israel, for water of impurity was not splashed on him. He shall be unclean; his uncleanness is still on him.
This is the law: a man, when he dies in a tent, every one coming to the tent and everyone who is in the tent shall be unclean seven days.
And every opened vessel on which there is no cover of cord, it is unclean.
And everyone who touches on the face of the field a slain one of the sword or a dying one or a bone of a man or a grave shall be unclean seven days.
And they shall take for the unclean one from the dust of the burning of the sin offering, and he shall put living water on it into a vessel.

And he shall take hyssop, and a clean man shall dip it in the water, and he shall sprinkle on the tent and on all the vessels and on the persons who were there, and on the one touching the bone or the slain one or the dying one or the grave.

And the clean one shall sprinkle on the unclean one on the third day and on the seventh day. And he shall purify him on the seventh day, and he shall wash his clothes and bathe in the water, and he will be clean in the evening.

And a man who is unclean and does not purify himself, that person shall be cut off from the midst of the assembly, for he has made the holy place of Yahweh unclean. Water of impurity was not splashed on him; he is unclean.

And it shall be for them for a statute of eternity. And the one sprinkling the water of the impurity shall wash his clothes, and the one touching the water of the impurity shall be unclean until the evening.
And everything that the unclean one touches shall be unclean, and the person touching it shall be unclean until the evening.’”
And the sons of Israel, all the congregation, came to the wilderness of Zin in the first month, and the people dwelt at Kadesh. And Miriam died there, and she was buried there.
And there was no water for the congregation, and they assembled against Moses and against Aaron.
And the people contended with Moses, and they said, saying, “If only we had expired when our brothers expired to the face of Yahweh!
And why have you brought the assembly of Yahweh to this wilderness to die there, we and our livestock?
And why did you bring us up from Egypt to bring us to this evil place? It is not a place of seed and fig and vine and pomegranate, and there is no water to drink!”
And Moses and Aaron went from the face of the assembly to the opening of the tent of meeting, and they fell on their faces. And the glory of Yahweh appeared to them.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“Take the staff and assemble the congregation, you and Aaron your brother, and you shall speak to the rock to their eyes, and it will give its water. And you shall bring out for them water from the rock, and you shall make the congregation and their livestock drink.”
And Moses took the staff from before the face of Yahweh, just as he commanded him.
And Moses and Aaron assembled the assembly to the face of the rock, and he said to them, “Please hear, the rebellious ones! From this rock shall we bring out water for you?”
And Moses raised his hand, and he struck the rock with his staff two times. And much water came out, and the congregation drank and their livestock.

And Yahweh said to Moses and to Aaron, “Because you did not believe in me to consecrate me to the eyes of the sons of Israel, therefore you will not bring this assembly to the land that I have given to them.”
They are the waters of Meribah, where the sons of Israel contended with Yahweh, and he showed himself to be holy in them.
And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom: “Thus says your brother Israel: ‘You, you know all of the hardship that has found us.
And our fathers went down to Egypt, and we dwelled in Egypt many days, and the Egyptians did evil to us and to our fathers.
And we cried out to Yahweh, and he heard our voice, and he sent a messenger, and he brought us out from Egypt. And behold, we are in Kadesh, a city of the edge of your border.

Please let us pass through your land. We will not pass through field or vineyard, and we will not drink water of a well. We will go on the road of the king. We will not turn right or left until we have passed through your border.’”
And Edom said to him, “You shall not pass through me, lest I come out to meet you with the sword.”

And the sons of Israel said to him, “We will go up on the highway. And if we drink your water, I and my livestock, then I will give their price. Only, there is no matter; let me pass through on my feet.”
And he said, “You shall not pass through.” And Edom came out to meet him with a heavy people and with a strong hand.
And Edom refused to give Israel passage through his border. And Israel turned away from him.
And they set out from Kadesh, and the sons of Israel, all of the congregation, came to Mount Hor.
And Yahweh said to Moses and to Aaron at Mount Hor, on the border of the land of Edom, saying,
“Aaron will be gathered to his people, for he will not come into the land that I have given to the sons of Israel, because you rebelled against my mouth at the waters of Meribah.
Take Aaron and Eleazar his son and bring them up to Mount Hor.
And strip Aaron of his garments and put them on Eleazar his son. And Aaron will be gathered and will die there.”
And Moses did just as Yahweh commanded. And they went up to Mount Hor to the eyes of all of the congregation.
And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments and put them on Eleazar his son. And Aaron died there on the top of the mountain. And Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.
And all of the congregation saw that Aaron had expired. And all of the house of Israel wept for Aaron 30 days.
And the Canaanite, the king of Arad, the dweller of the Negev, heard that Israel had come by the way of the Atharim, and he fought against Israel and took captive some of them as captives.
And Israel vowed a vow to Yahweh and said, “If giving, you give this people into my hand, then I will devote their cities.”
And Yahweh listened to the voice of Israel and gave the Canaanite, and he devoted them and their cities, and he called the name of the place Hormah.
And they set out from Mount Hor by the way of the Sea of Reeds to go around the land of Edom. And the soul of the people was short on the way,

and the people spoke against God and against Moses, “Why have you brought us up from Egypt to die in the wilderness? For there is no bread and there is no water, and our soul loathes the worthless bread.”
And Yahweh sent the serpents, the fiery ones, among the people, and they bit the people, and many people from Israel died.

And the people came to Moses and said, “We have sinned, for we have spoken against Yahweh and against you. Pray to Yahweh, and let him remove from upon us the serpent.” And Moses prayed on behalf of the people.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Make for yourself a fiery serpent and set it on a banner, and it will be that everyone who is bitten and sees it will live.”
And Moses made a serpent of bronze, and he set it on the banner. And it was that if a serpent bit a man and he looked to the serpent of bronze, then he lived.
And the sons of Israel set out and camped at Oboth.
And they set out from Oboth and camped at Iye Abarim in the wilderness that is on the face of Moab, from the rising of the sun.
From there they set out and camped at the wadi of Zered.

From there they set out and camped on the other side of the Arnon, which is in the wilderness going out from the border of the Amorite. For the Arnon is the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorite.
Therefore it is said in the Book of the Wars of Yahweh, “Waheb in Suphah and the wadis of the Arnon,
and the slope of the wadis that extends to the dwelling of Ar and leans to the border of Moab.”
And from there to Beer. It is the well where Yahweh said to Moses, “Gather the people, and I will give water to them.”
Then Israel sang this song: “Spring up, O well! Sing to it!
The well that the princes dug, the nobles of the people dug it, with the scepter, with their staffs.” And from the wilderness to Mattanah,
and from Mattanah to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
and from Bamoth to the valley that is in the field of Moab, the head of Pisgah, and it looks down over the face of the wasteland.
And Israel sent messengers to Sihon, king of the Amorite, saying,
“Let me pass through your land. We will not turn into a field or into a vineyard. We will not drink water of a well. We will go by the way of the king until we pass through your border.”

And Sihon did not give Israel permission to pass through his border, and Sihon gathered all his people and went out to meet Israel toward the wilderness, and he came to Jahaz and fought against Israel.
And Israel struck him by the mouth of the sword and possessed his land from the Arnon as far as the Jabbok, as far as the sons of Ammon, for the border of the sons of Ammon was strong.
And Israel took all these cities, and Israel dwelled in all the cities of the Amorite, in Heshbon and in all its daughters.
For Heshbon was the city of Sihon, king of the Amorite, and he had fought against the former king of Moab and had taken all his land from his hand as far as the Arnon.
Therefore the ones speaking in proverbs say, “Come to Heshbon! Let it be built and established, the city of Sihon!
For a fire went out from Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon. It consumed Ar of Moab, the lords of the high places of the Arnon.
Woe to you, Moab! You have perished, people of Chemosh! He has given his sons as fugitives and his daughters into captivity, to Sihon, king of the Amorite.
And we shot at them. Heshbon has perished as far as Dibon. And we made desolation as far as Nophah, which is as far as Medeba.”
And Israel dwelled in the land of the Amorite.
And Moses sent to spy out Jazer, and they captured its daughters and dispossessed the Amorite who was there.
And they turned and went up the way of Bashan. And Og, king of Bashan, went out to meet them, he and all his people, to the battle at Edrei.

And Yahweh said to Moses, “Do not fear him, for into your hand I have given him and all his people and his land. And you shall do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorite, who dwelled in Heshbon.”
And they struck him and his sons and all his people until there was no survivor remaining for him, and they possessed his land.
And the sons of Israel set out and camped in the plains of Moab, from the other side of the Jordan at Jericho.
And Balak the son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorite.
And Moab was extremely terrified because of the face of the people, because they were numerous. And Moab was sick with terror because of the face of the sons of Israel,
and Moab said to the elders of Midian, “Now the assembly will lick up all that is around us like an ox licks up the green of the field.” And Balak the son of Zippor was the king of Moab at that time,

and he sent messengers to Balaam the son of Beor at Pethor, which is near the River, the land of the sons of his people, to summon him, saying, “Behold, a people went out from Egypt. Behold, he has covered the eye of the land, and he is dwelling opposite me.

And now please go curse this people for me because they are more numerous than I. Perhaps I will be able to strike them and drive them out from the land. For I know whom you bless will be blessed, and whom you curse will be cursed.”
And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian went, and divination fees were in their hand, and they came to Balaam and spoke to him the words of Balak.
And he said to them, “Lodge here tonight, and I will bring you back a word, just as Yahweh speaks to me,” and the leaders of Moab stayed with Balaam.
And God came to Balaam and said, “Who are these men with you?”
And Balaam said to God, “Balak the son of Zippor, the king of Moab, has sent to me.
‘Behold, the people who have come out from Egypt and have covered the eye of the land! Now go curse them for me. Perhaps I will be able to fight them and drive them out.’”
And God said to Balaam, “You shall not go with them. You shall not curse the people, because they are blessed.”
And Balaam rose in the morning and said to the leaders of Balak, “Go to your land, because Yahweh has refused to grant me to go with you.”
And the leaders of Moab rose and came to Balak, and they said, “Balaam refused to come with us.”
And Balak added again and sent many leaders, and they were more honored than these.
And they came to Balaam and said to him, “Thus says Balak the son of Zippor, ‘Please do not refrain from coming to me,
for I will very greatly honor you, and I will do all that you say to me. And please go curse this people for me.’”
And Balaam answered and said to the servants of Balak, “If Balak gives to me his house full of silver and gold, I will not be able to go beyond the mouth of Yahweh my God to do small or big.
And now, you also please stay in this place tonight; and may I know what Yahweh will add to speak with me.”
And God came to Balaam at night and said to him, “If the men have come to summon you, rise and go with them, and only the word that I speak to you, it you shall do.”
And Balaam rose in the morning and saddled his female donkey and went with the leaders of Moab.

And the nose of God burned because he was going; and the angel of Yahweh stationed himself in the road as an adversary to him, and he was riding on his female donkey, and two of his servants were with him.

And the female donkey saw the angel of Yahweh stationed in the road, and his drawn sword was in his hand, and the female donkey turned aside from the road and went in the field. And Balaam struck the female donkey to turn her to the road.
And the angel of Yahweh stood in the narrow pass of the vineyards, a wall on this side and a wall on that side.
And the female donkey saw the angel of Yahweh, and she pressed herself to the wall, and she pressed the foot of Balaam to the wall. And he struck her again.
And the angel of Yahweh passed on again and stood in a narrow place where there was no road to turn right or left.
And the female donkey saw the angel of Yahweh and lay down under Balaam, and the nose of Balaam burned, and he struck the female donkey with the staff.
And Yahweh opened the mouth of the female donkey, and she said to Balaam, “What have I done to you that you struck me these three times?”
And Balaam said to the female donkey, “Because you acted mockingly with me! If only there was a sword in my hand! Surely I would slay you!”
And the female donkey said to Balaam, “Am I not your female donkey on which you have ridden from your continuance until this day? Have I ever been accustomed to doing thus to you?” And he said, “No.”
And Yahweh uncovered the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel of Yahweh stationed in the road and his drawn sword in his hand. And he bowed and bowed down his nose.

And the angel of Yahweh said to him, “On account of what have you struck your female donkey these three times? Behold, I myself have gone out as an adversary because your way has been perverse in front of me.
And the female donkey saw me and turned aside before my face these three times. If she had not turned aside from me, surely now even you I would have slain and kept her alive.”
And Balaam said to the angel of Yahweh, “I have sinned, for I did not know that you were stationed to meet me in the road. And now, if it is evil in your eyes, I will return to me.”
And the angel of Yahweh said to Balaam, “Go with the men, and only the word that I speak to you, it you shall speak.” And Balaam went with the leaders of Balak.
And Balak heard that Balaam had come, and he went out to meet him at a city of Moab, that is near the border of the Arnon, which is at the end of the border.
And Balak said to Balaam, “Did I not indeed send to you to summon you? Why did you not come to me? Am I truly not able to honor you?”
And Balaam said to Balak, “Behold, I have come to you. Am I now at all able to speak anything? The word that God puts in my mouth, it will I speak.”
And Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath Huzoth,
and Balak sacrificed cattle and sheep and sent to Balaam and to the leaders who were with him.
And it happened in the morning that Balak took Balaam and brought him up to the high place of Baal, and from there he saw the edge of the people.
And Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me in this place seven altars, and prepare for me in this place seven bulls and seven rams.”
And Balak did just as Balaam had said, and Balak and Balaam offered up a bull and a ram on the altar.

And Balaam said to Balak, “Station yourself beside your burnt offering, and I will go. Perhaps Yahweh will be met to meet me, and I will declare to you the word of what he shows me.” And he went to a bare high place.
And God met with Balaam, and he said to him, “I have arranged seven altars and offered up a bull and a ram on the altar.”
And Yahweh put a word in the mouth of Balaam and said, “Return to Balak and speak thus.”
And he returned to him, and behold, he was stationed beside his burnt offering, he and all the leaders of Moab.

And he lifted up his oracle and said, “Balak has led me from Aram, the king of Moab from the mountains of the east. ‘Go curse Jacob for me, and go denounce Israel.’ 8 How can I curse? God has not cursed. And how can I denounce? Yahweh has not denounced. 9 For from the head of the rocks I see him. And from the hills I look at him. Behold, the people dwells by itself and it does not reckon itself among the nations. 10 Who can count the dust of Jacob or number the fourth part of Israel? Let my life die the death of the upright ones, and let my end be like his!”
And Balak said to Balaam, “What have you done to me? I took you to curse my enemies, and behold, you have blessed indeed!”
And he answered and said, “Should I not be careful to speak it that Yahweh puts in my mouth?”
And Balak said to him, “Please go with me to another place from which you can see him. You will only see his edge, and you will not see all of him, and you will curse him for me from there.”
And he took him to the field of Zophim, to the head of the Pisgah, and he built seven altars and offered up a bull and a ram on the altar.
Then he said to Balak, “Station yourself here beside your burnt offering, and I will be met with there.”
And Yahweh met with Balaam and put a word in his mouth, and he said, “Return to Balak, and thus you shall speak.”
And he came to him, and behold, he was stationed beside his altar, and the leaders of Moab were with him; and Balak said to him, “What did Yahweh speak?”

And he lifted up his oracle and said, “Rise up, Balak, and listen. Give ear to me, son of Zippor. 19 God is not a man, that he would lie, Or a son of mankind, that he would change his mind. Has he said and not done, Or spoken and not made it arise?

Behold, I have received to bless. And he has blessed, and I cannot turn it back. 21 He has not looked at hardship in Jacob and he has not seen trouble in Israel. Yahweh his God is with him, and the shout of the king is with him.

God is bringing them out from Egypt like the horns of a wild ox for him. 23 For there is no omen against Jacob, and no divination against Israel. At this time it shall be said of Jacob and of Israel, ‘What God has done!’ 24 Look, a people rises like a lioness, and like a lion he lifts himself up. He will not lie down until he eats prey and drinks the blood of the pierced ones.”
And Balak said to Balaam, “Even do not curse him at all! Also, do not bless him at all!”
And Balaam answered and said to Balak, “Did I not speak to you, saying, ‘All that Yahweh speaks, it I must do?’”
And Balak said to Balaam, “Come now, I will take you to another place. Perhaps it will be right in the eyes of God, and you will curse him for me from there.”
And Balak took Balaam to the head of the Peor, looking down on the face of the wasteland.
And Balaam said to Balak, “Build for me in this place seven altars and prepare for me in this place seven bulls and seven rams.”
And Balak did just as Balaam had said, and he offered up a bull and a ram on the altar.
And Balaam saw that it was good in the eyes of Yahweh to bless Israel, and he did not go as time by time to meet omens, and he set his face to the wilderness.
And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel dwelling according to its tribes, and the Spirit of God was on him.

And he lifted up his discourse and said, “The declaration of Balaam the son of Beor, and the declaration of the man opened of eye, 4 the declaration of the hearer of the words of God, who sees the vision of the Almighty, falling down and opened of eyes: 5 How good are your tents, Jacob, your dwellings, Israel! 6 Like wadis they spread out, like gardens beside a river, like aloes Yahweh has planted, like cedars beside water. 7 Water will flow from his buckets, and his seed with much water. And his king will be higher than Agag, and his kingdom will lift itself up. 8 God is bringing him out from Egypt, like the horns of a wild ox for him. He will eat the nations, his enemies. And he will break their bones. And he will strike his arrows. 9 He crouched, he lay down like a lion, and like a lioness. Who will get him up? Those who bless you are blessed; and those who curse you are cursed.”
And the nose of Balak burned toward Balaam and he struck his palms, and Balak said to Balaam, “I called you to curse my enemies, and behold, you have repeatedly blessed these three times!
And now, flee for yourself to your place. I said I would honor you greatly, and behold, Yahweh has held you back from honor.”
And Balaam said to Balak, “Did I not also speak to the messengers that you sent to me, saying.
‘If Balak gives to me his house full of silver and gold, I will not be able to go beyond the mouth of Yahweh to do good or evil from my heart; what Yahweh speaks, it I must speak?’
And now, behold I am about to go to my people. Come, I will advise you what this people will do to your people in the future days.”

And he lifted up his discourse and said, “The declaration of Balaam the son of Beor, and the declaration of the man opened of eye, 16 the declaration of the hearer of the words of God, and the knower of the knowledge of the Most High, he sees the vision of the Almighty, falling down and opened of eyes: 17 I will see him, but not now; I will look at him, but not near. A star has marched from Jacob, and a rod has risen from Israel, and he will strike the sides of Moab and destroy all the sons of Seth. 18 And Edom will become a possession, and Seir will become a possession, his enemies. And Israel will act strongly. 19 And he will rule from Jacob, and he will destroy the survivor from a city.”
And he saw Amalek and lifted up his discourse and said, “The first of the nations is Amalek, and his end will be up to destruction.”

And he saw the Kenite, and he lifted up his discourse and said, “Your dwelling is permanent, and your nest is set in the cliff. 22 But the Kenite will be for burning. Until when will Assyria take you captive?”

And he lifted up his discourse and said, “Woe! Who can live apart from God establishing him? 24 And ships will be from the hand of Kittim, and they will afflict Asshur and will afflict Eber, and he, too, will be up to destruction.”
And Balaam got up and went and returned to his place, and Balak also went on his way.
And Israel dwelled in Shittim, and the people began to prostitute themselves with the daughters of Moab,
and they called the people to the sacrifices of their gods, and the people ate, and they worshipped their gods.
And Israel bound himself to Baal of Peor. And the nose of Yahweh burned against Israel.
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Take all the heads of the people and hang them up before Yahweh in front of the sun, and the burning of the nose of Yahweh may turn back from Israel.”
And Moses said to the judges of Israel, “Slay a man his men, the ones who bound themselves to Baal of Peor.”

And behold, a man from the sons of Israel came and brought near to his brothers the Midianite woman, before the eyes of Moses and before the eyes of all the congregation of the sons of Israel. And they were weeping at the opening of the tent of meeting.
And Phinehas the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, saw and rose up from the midst of the congregation, and took a spear in his hand.
And he came after the man of Israel into the domed tent, and he pierced the two of them, the man of Israel and the woman in her stomach. And the plague was stopped from on the sons of Israel.
And the ones who died by the plague were 24, 000.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“Phinehas the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, has turned my heat from on the sons of Israel by his being zealous with my zeal in the midst of them. And I will not make an end of the sons of Israel in my zeal.
Therefore say, ‘Behold, I am giving to him my covenant of peace.
And it will be a covenant of a priesthood of eternity for him and for his seed after him, because he was zealous for his God and he made atonement for the sons of Israel.’”
And the name of the man of Israel, the struck down one who was struck down with the Midianite woman, was Zimri the son of Salu, the leader of a house of a father of the Simeonites.
And the name of the woman, the struck down Midanite woman, was Kozbi the daughter of Zur, who was the head of a tribe of a house of a father in Midian.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Vex the Midianites and you shall strike them down.

For they were vexing you by their deceitfulness when they deceived you over the matter of Peor and over the matter of Kozbi the daughter of the leader of Midian, their sister, the one struck down on the plague day over the matter of Peor.”
And it happened after the plague that Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying,
“Lift up the heads of all the congregation of the sons of Israel from a son of 20 years and upward according to the house of their fathers, all who go out to war in Israel.”
And Moses and Eleazar the priest spoke with them in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan at Jericho, saying,
“From a son of 20 years and upward, just as Yahweh commanded Moses and the sons of Israel, the ones who came out from the land of Egypt.”
Reuben was the firstborn of Israel. The sons of Reuben: Hanok, the clan of the Hanokite; of Pallu, the clan of the Palluite;
of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronite; of Karmi, the clan of the Karmite.
These were the clans of the Reubenite. And their counted ones were 43, 730.
And the son of Pallu was Eliab.

And the sons of Eliab were Nemuel and Dathan and Abiram. This is Dathan and Abiram, the called ones of the congregation, who rebelled against Moses and against Aaron with the congregation of Korah when they rebelled against Yahweh.
And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and Korah when the congregation died, when the fire ate 250 men, and they became a sign.
And the sons of Korah did not die.
The sons of Simeon according to their clans: of Nemuel, the clan of the Nemuelite; of Jamin, the clan of the Jaminite; of Jakin, the clan of the Jakinite;
of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahite; of Shaul, the clan of the Shaulite.
These were the clans of the Simeonite, 22, 200.
The sons of Gad according to their clans: of Zephon, the clan of the Zephonite; of Haggi, the clan of the Haggite; of Shuni, the clan of the Shunite;
of Ozni, the clan of the Oznite; of Eri, the clan of the Erite;
of Arod, the clan of the Arodite; of Areli, the clan of the Arelite.
These were the clans of the sons of Gad according to their counted ones, 40, 500.
The sons of Judah were Er and Onan, and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
And the sons of Judah according to their clans were: of Shelah, the clan of the Shelanite; of Perez, the clan of the Perezite; of Zerah, the clan of the Zerahite.
And the sons of Perez were: of Hezron, the clan of the Hezronite; of Hamul, the clan of the Hamulite.
These were the clans of Judah according to their counted ones, 76, 500.
The sons of Issachar according to their clans: Tola, the clan of the Tolaite; of Puah, the clan of the Puite;
of Jashub, the clan of the Jashubite; of Shimron, the clan of the Shimronite.
These were the clans of Issachar according to their counted ones, 64, 300.
The sons of Zebulun according to their clans: of Sered, the clan of the Seredite; of Elon, the clan of the Elonite; of Jahleel, the clan of the Jahleelite.
These were the clans of the Zebulunite according to their counted ones, 60, 500.
The sons of Joseph according to their clans were Manasseh and Ephraim.
The sons of Manasseh: of Makir, the clan of the Makirite (and Makir fathered Gilead); of Gilead, the clan of the Gileadite.

These were the sons of Gilead: Iezer, the clan of the Iezerite; of Helek, the clan of the Helekite; 31 and Asriel, the clan of the Asrielite; and Shechem, the clan of the Shechemite; 32 and Shemida, the clan of the Shemidaite; and Hepher, the clan of the Hepherite.
And there were no sons for Zelophehad the son of Hepher, but daughters. And the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milkah, and Tirzah.
These were the clans of Manasseh and their counted ones, 52, 700.
These were the sons of Ephraim according to their clans: of Shuthelah, the clan of the Shuthelahite; of Beker, the clan of the Bekerite; of Tahan, the clan of the Tahanite.
And these were the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the clan of the Eranite.
These were the clans of the sons of Ephraim according to their counted ones, 32, 500. These were the sons of Joseph according to their clans.

The sons of Benjamin according to their clans: of Bela, the clan of the Belaite; of Ashbel, the clan of the Ashbelite; of Ahiram, the clan of the Ahiramite; 39 of Shephupham, the clan of the Shuphamite; of Hupham, the clan of the Huphamite.
And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: the clan of the Ardite; of Naaman, the clan of the Naamite.
These were the sons of Benjamin according to their clans. And their counted ones were 45, 600.
These were the sons of Dan according to their clans: of Shuham, the clan of the Shuhamite. These were the clans of Dan according to their clans.
All the clans of the Shuhamite according to their counted ones were 64, 400.
The sons of Asher according to their clans: of Imnah, the clan of the Imnite; of Ishvi, the clan of the Ishvite; of Beriah, the clan of the Beriite.
The sons of Beriah: of Heber, the clan of the Heberite; of Malkiel, the clan of the Malkielite.
And the name of the daughter of Asher was Serah.
These were the clans of the sons of Asher according to their counted ones, 53, 400.
The sons of Naphtali according to their clans: of Jahzeel, the clan of the Jahzeelite; of Guni, the clan of the Gunite; 49 of Jezer, the clan of the Jezerite; of Shillem, the clan of the Shillemite.
These were the clans of Naphtali according to their clans. And their counted ones were 45, 400.
These were the counted ones of the sons of Israel: 601, 730.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“The land shall be apportioned to these ones as an inheritance by the number of the names.
To the numerous you shall increase his inheritance, and to the small you shall lessen his inheritance. His inheritance shall be given, a man according to the mouth of his counted ones.
Surely the land shall be apportioned by lots. They shall inherit according to the names of the tribes of their fathers.
His inheritance shall be apportioned in accordance with the mouth of the lot, between the numerous and the small.”
And these were the counted ones of the Levite according to their clans: of Gershon, the clan of the Gershonite; of Kohath, the clan of the Kohathite; of Merari, the clan of the Merarite.
These were the clans of the Levite: the clan of the Libnite, the clan of the Hebronite, the clan of the Mahlite, the clan of the Mushite, the clan of the Korahite. And Kohath fathered Amram.
And the name of the wife of Amram was Jochebed, a daughter of Levi, she who was born to Levi in Egypt. And she bore to Amram Aaron and Moses and Miriam their sister.
And to Aaron were born Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
And Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unfamiliar fire before the face of Yahweh.

And their counted ones were 23, 000, every male from a son of a month and upward. But they were not counted in the midst of the sons of Israel because an inheritance in the midst of the sons of Israel was not given to them.
These are the counted ones of Moses and Eleazar the priest when they counted the sons of Israel in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan at Jericho.
And among these there was not a man from the counted ones of Moses and Aaron the priest when they counted the sons of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.
For Yahweh had said to them, “You shall surely die in the wilderness and a man shall not remain from them, except Caleb the son of Jephunneh and Joshua the son of Nun.”

And the daughters of Zelophehad the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir the son of Manasseh, of the clans of Manasseh the son of Joseph came near. And these were the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah and Hoglah and Milkah and Tirzah.
And they stood before the face of Moses and before the face of Eleazar the priest and before the face of the leaders and all the congregation at the opening of the tent of meeting, saying,

“Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not in the midst of the congregation who assembled against Yahweh with the congregation of Korah, but he died in his sin, and there were no sons for him.
Why should the name of our father be withdrawn from the midst of his clan because there is no son for him? Give to us a possession in the midst of the brothers of our father.”
And Moses brought near their case before the face of Yahweh.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

“The daughters of Zelophehad are speaking rightly. You shall certainly give to them the possession of an inheritance in the midst of the brothers of their father, and you shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them.
And you shall speak to the sons of Israel, saying, ‘If a man dies and there is no son for him, then you shall cause his inheritance to pass over to his daughter.
And if there is no daughter for him, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.
And if there are no brothers for him, then you shall give his inheritance to the brothers of his father.

And if there are no brothers for his father, then you shall give his inheritance to his blood-relative, the nearest to him from his clan, and he shall take possession of it. And it shall be for the sons of Israel as a statute of judgment, just as Yahweh has commanded Moses.’”
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Go up to this mountain of Abarim, and see the land that I have given to the sons of Israel.
And you shall see it and be gathered to your people, also you, just as Aaron your brother was gathered,

because in the wilderness of Zin, in the quarrel of the congregation, you rebelled against my mouth to regard me as holy at the waters before their eyes.” These are the waters of Meribah Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.
And Moses spoke to Yahweh, saying,
“May Yahweh, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation

who will go out before the face of them and who will come before the face of them, and who will take them out and who will bring them in, and the congregation of Yahweh will not be like sheep that have no shepherd for them.”
And Yahweh said to Moses, “Take for yourself Joshua the son of Nun, a man in whom is a spirit, and lay your hand on him.
And you shall stand him before the face of Eleazar the priest and before the face of all the congregation, and you shall command him before their eyes.
And you shall give some of your authority on him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel will hear.

And he shall stand before the face of Eleazar the priest, and he shall ask for him by the judgment of the Urim before the face of Yahweh. In accordance with his mouth he shall go out and in accordance with his mouth he shall come in, he and all the sons of Israel with him and all the congregation.”
And Moses did just as Yahweh had commanded him, and he took Joshua and stood him before the face of Eleazar the priest and before the face of all the congregation,
and he laid his hands on him, and he commanded him, just as Yahweh had spoken by the hand of Moses.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Command the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘You shall be careful to offer to me at its appointed time my offering, my food for my offerings made by fire, an aroma of my soothing.’
And you shall say to them, ‘This is the offering made by fire that you shall offer to Yahweh: unblemished male lambs, sons of a year, two for the day, a burnt offering continually.
One male lamb you shall do in the morning, and the second male lamb you shall do between the evenings,
and the tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering, mixed with the fourth of a hin of beaten oil.
It is a burnt offering of continuity, the one done at Mount Sinai for an aroma of soothing, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
And its drink offering shall be the fourth of a hin for one male lamb. You shall pour out in the holy place a drink offering of intoxicating drink to Yahweh.
And you shall do the second male lamb between the evenings. You shall do like the grain offering of the morning and its drink offering, an offering made by fire, an aroma of soothing to Yahweh.
And on the Sabbath day: two unblemished male lambs, sons of a year, and two fine flour as a grain offering mixed with the oil, and its drink offering
are the burnt offering of the Sabbath by its Sabbath, in addition to the burnt offering of the continuity and its drink offering.
And at the heads of your months, you shall offer a burnt offering to Yahweh: two bulls, sons of cattle, and one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished;
and three fine flour as a grain offering mixed with the oil for one bull, and two fine flour as a grain offering mixed with the oil for the one ram,
and a tenth, a tenth fine flour as a grain offering mixed with the oil for one male lamb. It is a burnt offering, an aroma of soothing, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.

And their drink offerings shall be half of a hin of wine for the bull and a third of a hin for the ram and a fourth of a hin for the male lamb. This shall be the burnt offering of the month by its month for the months of the year.
And one buck of the goats shall be done as a sin offering to Yahweh in addition to the burnt offering of the continuity and its drink offering.
And in the first month, on day 14 of the month is the Passover of Yahweh.
And on day 15 of this month shall be a feast. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
On the first day shall be a convocation of holiness. You shall not do any work of labor,
and you shall offer an offering made by fire, a burnt offering to Yahweh: two bulls, sons of cattle, and one ram, and seven male lambs, sons of a year. They shall be unblemished for you.
And their grain offering: you shall do fine flour mixed with the oil, three for the bull and two for the ram.
A tenth, a tenth you shall do for one male lamb of the seven male lambs,
and one male goat as the sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.
You shall do these apart from the burnt offering of the morning, which is for the burnt offering of the continuity.

You shall do like these for the day, seven days, the food of the offering made by fire, an aroma of soothing to Yahweh. It shall be done in addition to the burnt offering of the continuity and its drink offering.
And on the seventh day there shall be a convocation of holiness for you. You shall not do any work of labor.
And on the day of the firstfruits, when you offer the new grain offering to Yahweh in your festival of weeks, there shall be a convocation of holiness for you. You shall not do any work of labor.
And you shall offer a burnt offering as an aroma of soothing to Yahweh: two bulls, sons of cattle, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year,
And their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with the oil, three for one bull, two for the one ram,
a tenth, a tenth for one male lamb of the seven of the male lambs,
one buck of the goats to make atonement for yourselves.
You shall do apart from the burnt offering of the continuity and its grain offering (they shall be unblemished for you) and their drink offerings.’”
“And in the seventh month, on the first of the month, there shall be a convocation of holiness for you. You shall not do any work of labor. It shall be a day of horn blowing for you.
And you shall do a burnt offering for an aroma of soothing to Yahweh: one bull, a son of cattle, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished.
And their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with the oil, three for the bull, two for the ram,
and one for one male lamb of the seven male lambs.
And one buck of the goats shall be a sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.

It is apart from the burnt offering of the month and its grain offering, and the burnt offering of the continuity and its grain offering, and their drink offerings according to their ordinance, for an aroma of soothing, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
And on the tenth of this seventh month there shall be a convocation of holiness for you. And you shall afflict your lives. You shall not do any work.
And you shall offer a burnt offering to Yahweh, an aroma of soothing: one bull, a son of cattle, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year. They shall be unblemished for you.
And their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with the oil, three for the bull, two for the one ram,
a tenth, a tenth for the one male lamb, for the seven male lambs.
One buck of the goats shall be a sin offering, apart from the sin offering of the atonement and the burnt offering of the continuity and its grain offering and their drink offerings.
And on day 15 of the seventh month there shall be for you a convocation of holiness. You shall not do any work of labor, and you shall feast a feast to Yahweh seven days.
And you shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, an aroma of soothing to Yahweh, 13 bulls, sons of cattle, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year. They shall be unblemished.
And their grain offering shall be fine flour mixed with the oil, three for one bull for the 13 bulls, two for one ram for the two rams,
and a tenth, a tenth for one male lamb for the 14 male lambs.
And one buck of the goats shall be a sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
And on the second day: 12 bulls, sons of cattle, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the male lambs shall be by their number according to the ordinance.
And one buck of the goats shall be a sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
And on the third day: 11 bulls, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the male lambs shall be by their number according to the ordinance,
and one male goat of the sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity and its grain offering and its drink offering.
And on the fourth day: 10 bulls, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be by their number according to the ordinance.
And one buck of the goats shall be a sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
And on the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be by their number according to the ordinance,
and one male goat of a sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, and its grain offering, and its drink offering.
And on the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be by their number according to the ordinance,
and one male goat of a sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, its grain offering, and its drink offerings.
And on the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, 14 male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished,
and their grain offering and their drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the male lambs, shall be by their number according to their ordinance,
and one male goat of the sin offering, apart from the burnt offering of the continuity, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
On the eighth day an assembly shall be for you. You shall not do any work of labor.
And you shall offer a burnt offering, an offering made by fire, an aroma of soothing to Yahweh: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs, sons of a year, unblemished.
Their grain offering and their drink offerings, for the bull, for the ram, and for the male lambs, shall be by their number according to the ordinance,
and one male goat of the sin offering apart from the burnt offering of the continuity and its grain offering and its drink offering.

You shall do these for Yahweh at your appointed times, apart from your vows and your voluntary offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your grain offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.”
And Moses spoke to the sons of Israel according to all that Yahweh had commanded Moses.
And Moses spoke to the heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel, saying, “This is the word that Yahweh has commanded:
When a man vows a vow to Yahweh or swears an oath to bind a binding vow on his life, he shall not profane his word. He shall do according to all that came out from his mouth.
And if a woman vows a vow to Yahweh and binds a binding vow in the house of her father, in her youth,

and her father hears her vow and her binding vow that she bound on her life, and her father is silent to her, then all her vows shall stand, and every binding vow that she has bound on her life shall stand.
And if her father forbids her on the day of his hearing, any of her vows and her binding vows that she bound on her life shall not stand, and Yahweh will forgive her, because her father forbade her.
And if she ever belongs to a man, and her vows are on her, or thoughtless speech of her lips that she has bound is on her life,
and her husband hears on the day of his hearing and is silent to her, then her vows shall stand, and her binding vows that she bound on her life shall stand.
And if her husband forbids her on the day of his hearing, then he breaks her vow which is on her and the thoughtless speech of her lips that she bound on her life, and Yahweh will forgive her.
And the vow of a widow and a driven out woman, all that she binds on her life shall stand on her.
And if she vowed or bound a binding vow on her life by an oath in the house of her husband,
and her husband hears and is silent to her, he does not forbid her, then all her vows shall stand, and every binding vow that she bound on her life shall stand.

And if her husband ever breaks them on the day of his hearing, all that went out of her lips for her vows and for the binding vow of her life shall not stand. Her husband has broken them, and Yahweh will forgive her.
Every vow and every oath of a binding vow to afflict her life, her husband may make it stand, and her husband may break it.

And if her husband is wholly silent to her from day to day, then he will make all her vows or all her binding vows which are on her stand. He made them stand because he was silent to her on the day of his hearing.
And if he ever breaks them after his hearing, then he will bear her iniquity.”
These are the statutes that Yahweh commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter in her youth in the house of her father.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Take vengeance, the vengeance of the sons of Israel, from with the Midianites. Afterward you shall be gathered to your people.”
And Moses spoke to the people, saying, “Equip yourselves, some of the men with you, for the war. And they will be against Midian to give the vengeance of Yahweh on Midian.
You shall send to the war for all the tribes of Israel, a thousand for a tribe, a thousand for a tribe.”
And a thousand for a tribe were provided from the thousands of Israel, 12, 000 equipped for battle.
And Moses sent them to the war, a thousand for a tribe, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest to the war. And the vessels of holiness and the blasting trumpets were in his hand.
And they waged war against Midian, just as Yahweh had commanded Moses, and they slew every male.
And they slew the kings of Midian with their pierced ones: Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, the five kings of Midian; and they slew Balaam the son of Beor by the sword.
And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their children, and they plundered all their animals and all their livestock and all their wealth.
And they burned with the fire all their cities with their dwellings and all their encampments.
And they took all the spoil and all the booty of human and of animal.

And they brought to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the congregation of the sons of Israel, the captives and the booty and the spoil to the camp at the plains of Moab, which are beside the Jordan at Jericho.
And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the congregation went out to meet them at the outside of the camp.
And Moses was furious with the appointed ones of the army, the leaders of the thousands and the leaders of the hundreds, the ones coming from the battle of the war.
And Moses said to them, “Have you let every woman live?
Look, these were by the word of Balaam for the sons of Israel to commit unfaithfulness against Yahweh concerning the matter of Peor, and the plague was among the congregation of Yahweh.
And now, slay every male among the children and slay every woman who has known a man by the bed of a male.
And keep alive for yourselves every child among the women who has not known the bed of a male.
And you, camp outside of the camp seven days, all who have slain a person and all who have touched a pierced one. Purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, you and your captives,
and you shall purify every garment and every vessel of skin and every work of goats and every vessel of wood.”
And Eleazar the priest said to the men of the army who had gone to the battle, “This is a statute of the law that Yahweh has commanded Moses:
Only the gold and the silver, the bronze, the iron, the tin, and the lead,

every thing that can come through the fire you shall pass through the fire, and it will become clean. However, it shall be purified with the water of purification, and all that can not come through the fire you shall pass through the water.
And you shall wash your garments on the seventh day, and you will be clean, and afterward you may enter into the camp.”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Lift up the head of the booty of the captives, of human and of animal, you and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the fathers of the congregation.
And you shall divide the booty between the wielders of the war who went out to the battle and all the congregation.
And you shall raise a tax for Yahweh from with the men of the war who went out to the battle, one life from 500, from human and from cattle and from the donkeys and from the flock.
You shall take from half of them and give the contribution of Yahweh to Eleazar the priest.

And from the half of the sons of Israel you shall take one taken from the 50, from human, from cattle, from the donkeys and from the flock, from every animal, and you shall give them to the Levites, the keepers of the keeping of the Dwelling of Yahweh.”
And Moses and Eleazar the priest did just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.
And the booty, the remainder of the plunder that the people of the army plundered, was 675, 000 sheep,
and 72, 000 cattle,
and 61, 000 donkeys,
and human lives, from the women who had not known the bed of a male, every life, 32, 000.
And the half of the portion of the ones who went out with the army: the number of the sheep was 337, 500.
And the tax for Yahweh from the flock was 675.
And the cattle were 36, 000 and their tax for Yahweh was 72.
And the donkeys were 30, 500, and their tax for Yahweh was 61.
And the human lives were 16, 000, and their tax for Yahweh was 32 lives.
And Moses gave the tax of the contribution of Yahweh to Eleazar the priest, just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.
And from the half of the sons of Israel whom Moses had divided from the men who waged war,
and the half of the congregation from the flock was 337, 500,
and 36, 000 cattle,
and 30, 500 donkeys,
and 16, 000 human lives.

And Moses took from the half of the sons of Israel the taken, one from the 50, from human and from animal, and he gave them to the Levites, the keepers of the keeping of the Dwelling of Yahweh, just as Yahweh had commanded Moses.
And the appointed ones who were for the thousands of the army, the leaders of the thousands and the leaders of the hundreds, came near to Moses.
And they said to Moses, “Your servants have lifted up the heads of the men of the war who were in our hand, and not a man from us is missing.

And we have offered the offering of Yahweh, a man what he found of the objects of gold, armlet, and bracelet, signet ring, earring, and ornament, to make atonement for our lives before the face of Yahweh.”
And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from with them, every object of craft.
And all the gold of the contribution which they raised to Yahweh from with the leaders of the thousands and from with the leaders of the hundreds was 16, 750 shekels.
The men of the army had plundered, a man for himself.

And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from with the leaders of the thousands and the hundreds, and they brought it to the tent of meeting, a memorial for the sons of Israel before the face of Yahweh.
And the livestock of the sons of Reuben and of the sons of Gad was great, very numerous. And they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, and behold, the place was a place of livestock.
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came and said to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the leaders of the congregation, saying,
“Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Sebam, and Nebo, and Beon
are the land that Yahweh struck before the face of the congregation of Israel. It is a land of livestock, and livestock belongs to your servants.”
And they said, “If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as a possession. Do not make us cross over the Jordan.”
And Moses said to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben, “Will your brothers go to the war and you yourselves will dwell here?
And why do you discourage the heart of the sons of Israel from crossing over into the land that Yahweh has given to them?
Thus your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh Barnea to see the land.
And they went up as far as the wadi of Eshkol, and they saw the land and discouraged the heart of the sons of Israel to not enter into the land that Yahweh had given to them.
And the nose of Yahweh burned on that day, and he swore, saying,
‘If the men who went up from Egypt, from a son of 20 years and upward, will see the ground which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob! For they are not fully after me,
except Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua the son of Nun, because they are fully after Yahweh.’
And the nose of Yahweh burned against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness 40 years until the finishing of all the generation who had done evil in the eyes of Yahweh.
And behold, you have risen up in place of your fathers, a brood of sinful men, to add more to the burning of the nose of Yahweh toward Israel.
If you turn from after him, then he will yet again leave him in the wilderness, and you will have destroyed all this people.”
And they came near to him and said, “Here we will build walls of the flock for our livestock and cities for our children.

And we ourselves will be equipped for war, hurrying before the face of the sons of Israel until when we have brought them into their place. And our children will dwell in the cities of the fortification because of the face of those who dwell in the land.
We will not return to our houses until the sons of Israel get an inheritance for themselves, a man his inheritance.
For we will not inherit with them from across of the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance came to us from beyond the Jordan toward the sunrise.”
And Moses said to them, “If you do this thing, if you equip yourselves before the face of Yahweh for the war,
then every man equipped for war shall cross over the Jordan for you before the face of Yahweh, until he has dispossessed his enemy from his face

and the land is subdued before the face of Yahweh, then afterward you may return. And you will be free of obligation from Yahweh and from Israel, and this land will be for you for a possession before the face of Yahweh.
And if you do not do so, behold, you will have sinned against Yahweh, and know that your sin will find you.
Build for yourselves cities for your children and walls for your livestock, and you shall do what has gone out from your mouth.”
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben spoke to Moses, saying, “Your servants will do just as my master is commanding.
Our children, our wives, our livestock, and all our animals will be there in the cities of the Gilead.
And your servants will cross over, every man equipped for war of the army, before the face of Yahweh for the war, just as my master has spoken.”
And Moses commanded Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel concerning them.

And Moses said to them, “If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben cross over the Jordan with you, every man equipped for the war before the face of Yahweh, and the land is subdued before your faces, then you will give to them the land of the Gilead as a possession.
And if the men equipped for war do not cross over with you, then they will take possession in your midst in the land of Canaan.”
And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying, “What Yahweh has spoken to your servants, so we will do.
We will cross over, the men equipped for war, before the face of Yahweh into the land of Canaan. And with us will be the possession of our inheritance from beyond the Jordan.”

And Moses gave to them, to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben, and to the half of the tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon the king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og the king of the Bashan, the land of its cities with the borders of the cities of the surrounding land.
And the sons of Gad built Dibon and Ataroth and Aroer
and Atroth Shophan and Jazer and Jogbehah
and Beth Nimrah and Beth Haran, the cities of fortification and the walls of the flock.
And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon and Elealeh and Kiriathaim
and Nebo and Baal Meon (names were changed) and Sibmah. And they called the names of the cities that they built by names.
And the sons of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead and captured it and dispossessed the Amorites who were in it.
And Moses gave the Gilead to Machir the son of Manasseh, and he dwelled in it.
And Jair the son of Manasseh went and captured their tent villages, and he called them Havvoth Jair.
And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its daughters. And he called it Nobah, by his name.
These are the journeys of the sons of Israel who went out from the land of Egypt according to their tribes by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
And Moses wrote down their departure points according to their tribes in accordance with the mouth of Yahweh, and these are their journeys according to their departure points.
And they set out from Rameses on the first month, on day 15 of the first month, from the day after the Passover the sons of Israel went out with a raised hand before the eyes of all the Egyptians.
And the Egyptians were burying those whom Yahweh had struck down, all the firstborn. And Yahweh enacted judgment against their gods.
And the sons of Israel set out from Rameses and camped at Succoth.
And they set out from Succoth and camped at Etham, which is at the edge of the wilderness.
And they set out from Etham and turned back to Pi Hahiroth, which is on the face of Baal Zephon, and they camped before the face of Migdol.
And they set out from the face of Hahiroth and passed through the midst of the sea to the wilderness, and they went a distance of three days in the wilderness of Etham and camped at Marah.
And they set out from Marah and came to Elim. And at Elim were 12 springs of water and 70 palm trees. And they camped there.
And they set out from Elim and camped beside the Sea of Reeds.
And they set out from the Sea of Reeds and camped in the wilderness of Sin.
And they set out from the wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah.
And they set out from Dophkah and camped at Alush.
And they set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim, and there was no water there for the people to drink.
And they set out from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai.
And they set out from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth Hattaavah.
And they set out from Kibroth Hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
And they set out from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.
And they set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon Perez.
And they set out from Rimmon Perez and camped at Libnah.
And they set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah.
And they set out from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.
And they set out from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.
And they set out from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah.
And they set out from Haradah and camped at Makheloth.
And they set out from Makheloth and camped at Tahath.
And they set out from Tahath and camped at Terah.
And they set out from Terah and camped at Mithkah.
And they set out from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah.
And they set out from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.
And they set out from Moseroth and camped at Bene Jaakan.
And they set out from Bene Jaakan and camped at Hor Haggidgad.
And they set out from Hor Haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah.
And they set out from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah.
And they set out from Abronah and camped at Ezion Geber.
And they set out from Ezion Geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin (it is Kadesh).
And they set out from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, at the edge of the land of Edom.

And Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor in accordance with the mouth of Yahweh and died there in the fortieth year of the going out of the sons of Israel from the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on 1 of the month.
And Aaron was a son of 123 years when he died at Mount Hor.
And the Canaanite, the king of Arad (and he was dwelling in the Negev in the land of Canaan), heard when the sons of Israel came.
And they set out from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.
And they set out from Zalmonah and camped at Punon.
And they set out from Punon and camped at Oboth.
And they set out from Oboth and camped at Iye Abarim, on the border of Moab.
And they set out from Iye and camped at Dibon Gad.
And they set out from Dibon Gad and camped at Almon Diblathaim.
And they set out from Almon Diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim, before the face of Nebo.
And they set out from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan at Jericho.
And they camped beside the Jordan, from Beth Jeshimoth to Abel Shittim in the plains of Moab.
And Yahweh spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab beside the Jordan at Jericho, saying,
“Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘When you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

then you shall dispossess all those who dwell in the land from your face, and you shall destroy all their carved figures, and you shall destroy all the figures of their molten metal, and you shall destroy all their high places.
And you shall take possession of the land and dwell in it, because I have given the land to you to possess it.

And you shall inherit for yourselves the land by lot, according to your clans. You shall increase his inheritance for the numerous, and you shall decrease his inheritance for the few. To there where the lot goes out for him, to him it will belong. According to the tribes of your fathers you shall inherit for yourselves.

And if you do not dispossess those who dwell in the land from your face, then whom you leave from them will be as thorns in your eyes and as pricks in your sides, and they shall vex you on the land that you are about to dwell in.
And it will happen that I will do to you just as I had intended to do to them.’”
And Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
“Command the sons of Israel and say to them, ‘When you enter into the land of Canaan, this is the land that has fallen to you as an inheritance, the land of Canaan and its borders,
and the corner of the south will be for you from the wilderness of Zin beside the hands of Edom. And the border of the south will be for you from the end of the Sea of Salt eastward.

And the border will turn for you from the south to the ascent of Akrabbim and cross over to Zin, and its outer limits will be from the south to Kadesh Barnea, and it will go out to Hazar Addar and cross over to Azmon.
And the border will turn from Azmon toward the wadi of Egypt, and its outer limits will be to the sea.
And the border of the west, it will be for you the Great Sea and the border. This will be for you the border of the west.
And this will be for you the border of the north, from the Great Sea you shall mark out for you to Mount Hor.
